title.zh: 卷五十四 楊震列傳
title.raw: 卷五十四 楊震列傳 Volume 54: Biography of Yang Zhen

sentences.zh: 後漢書卷五十四 楊震列傳 第四十四

translations.literal: Book of Later Han, Volume 54, Biographies: Yang Zhen, forty-fourth in the series.
translations.idiomatic: Book of Later Han, Volume 54: Biography of Yang Zhen (biographical series 44).

sentences.zh: 子秉孫賜曾孫彪玄孫修*

translations.literal: Son Bing; grandson Ci; great-grandson Biao; great-great-grandson Xiu.*
translations.idiomatic: His line continued through son Bing, grandson Ci, great-grandson Biao, and great-great-grandson Xiu.*

translations.literal: 1.3.1 Biao's son Xiu.
translations.idiomatic: 1.3.1 Yang Xiu, son of Yang Biao.

sentences.zh: 楊震字伯起，弘農華陰人也。

translations.literal: Yang Zhen, courtesy name Boqi, was a man of Huayin in Hongnong.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Zhen, styled Boqi, was a native of Huayin in Hongnong commandery.

sentences.zh: 八世祖喜，高祖時有功，封赤泉侯。

translations.literal: His eighth-generation ancestor Xi rendered merit in the time of Emperor Gaozu and was enfeoffed as Marquis of Chiquan.
translations.idiomatic: Eight generations back, his ancestor Xi had earned distinction under Han Emperor Gaozu and received the title Marquis of Chiquan.

sentences.zh: [1]高祖敞，昭帝時為丞相，封安平侯。

translations.literal: [1] His remote ancestor Chang served as Chancellor under Emperor Zhao and was enfeoffed as Marquis of Anping.
translations.idiomatic: [1] A more remote forebear, Chang, served as chancellor under Emperor Zhao and was enfeoffed as Marquis of Anping.

sentences.zh: 父寶，[二]習歐陽尚書。

translations.literal: His father Bao, [two] studied the Ouyang school's Documents.
translations.idiomatic: His father, Bao, was trained in the Ouyang tradition of the Book of Documents.

sentences.zh: 哀﹑平之世，隱居教授。

translations.literal: In the reigns of Ai and Ping he lived in seclusion and taught.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperors Ai and Ping he withdrew from public life and taught privately.

sentences.zh: 居攝二年，與兩龔﹑蔣詡俱征，遂遁逃，不知所處。

translations.literal: In the second year of the Juzshe era he was summoned together with the two Gongs and Jiang Xu, then fled and hid; his whereabouts were unknown.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of Wang Mang's regency he was called to court with Gong Sheng, Gong She, and Jiang Xu; he fled into hiding and disappeared.

sentences.zh: [三]光武高其節。

translations.literal: [Three] Emperor Guangwu esteemed his integrity.
translations.idiomatic: [3] Emperor Guangwu admired his moral resolve.

sentences.zh: 建武中，公車特徵，老病不到，卒於家。

translations.literal: During Jianwu he was specially summoned by imperial carriage; old age and illness prevented him from coming, and he died at home.
translations.idiomatic: In the Jianwu era the court summoned him by special edict, but citing age and illness he never took office and died at home.

sentences.zh: 注[二]續齊諧記曰：「寶年九歲時，至華陰山北，見一黃雀為鴟梟所搏，墜於樹下，為螻蟻所困。

translations.literal: [Two] The Supplementary Qi Xie Ji records: "When Bao was nine years old he went north of Mount Huayin and saw a yellow sparrow seized by an owl, fallen beneath a tree and trapped by ants."
translations.idiomatic: [2] The Supplementary Qi Xie Ji relates that at nine Bao went north of Mount Huayin and found a yellow sparrow snatched by a great horned owl, fallen to the ground and beset by ants.

sentences.zh: 寶取之以歸，置巾箱中，唯食黃花，百餘日毛羽成，乃飛去。

translations.literal: Bao took it home, put it in a cloth box, fed it only yellow flowers; after more than a hundred days its feathers grew complete, then it flew away.
translations.idiomatic: He carried it home, kept it in a cloth-lined box, fed it yellow blossoms alone; after a hundred-odd days it was fully fledged and flew off.

sentences.zh: 其夜有黃衣童子向寶再拜曰：『我西王母使者，君仁愛救拯，實感成濟。

translations.literal: That night a boy in yellow robes bowed twice to Bao and said: 'I am an envoy of the Queen Mother of the West; your benevolence in saving me truly moved me to completion and relief.
translations.idiomatic: That night a child in yellow appeared, bowed twice to Bao, and said, 'I am a messenger of the Queen Mother of the West; your kindness in rescuing me has brought me through.'

sentences.zh: 』以白環四枚與寶：『令君子孫潔白，位登三事，當如此環矣。

translations.literal: ' He gave Bao four white jade rings: 'May your lord's descendants be pure as these rings and rise to the Three Offices—such shall be their whiteness.'
translations.idiomatic: ' He presented four white jade rings: 'May your descendants remain unstained in honor and reach the three highest offices—pure as these rings.'

sentences.zh: 』」注[三]龔勝字君賓，龔捨字君倩，蔣詡字符卿，並以高節著名。

translations.literal: '" [Three] Gong Sheng, courtesy Junbin; Gong She, courtesy Junqian; Jiang Xu, courtesy Yuanqing—all were famed for lofty integrity.
translations.idiomatic: '" [3] Gong Sheng (Junbin), Gong She (Junqian), and Jiang Xu (Yuanqing) were all celebrated for their upright conduct.

sentences.zh: 見前書。

translations.literal: See the Former Han history.
translations.idiomatic: See the accounts in the Book of Han.

sentences.zh: 震少好學，受歐陽尚書於太常桓郁，明經博覽，無不窮究。

translations.literal: Zhen from youth loved learning; he received the Ouyang Documents from Huan Yu, Chamberlain for Ceremonials; he clarified the classics and read widely, leaving nothing unexamined.
translations.idiomatic: From boyhood Yang Zhen loved study. He learned the Ouyang Book of Documents from Huan Yu, the Chamberlain for Ceremonials, mastered the canonical texts, and read so broadly that nothing escaped his scrutiny.

sentences.zh: 諸儒為之語曰：「關西孔子楊伯起。

translations.literal: The scholars made a saying of him: 'West of the Passes, Confucius is Yang Boqi.
translations.idiomatic: Scholars nicknamed him: 'West of the passes, the Confucius is Yang Boqi.'

sentences.zh: 」常客居於湖，[一]不荅州郡禮命數十年，[二]觿人謂之晚暮，而震志愈篤。

translations.literal: ' He often lodged as a guest at Hu, [one] and for decades did not respond to provincial or commandery courtesy summons; [two] locals called him past his prime, yet Zhen's resolve grew firmer.
translations.idiomatic: ' He often lived as a guest at Lake [town], [1] declining provincial and commandery appointments for decades; [2] neighbors said he had missed his moment, but his purpose only deepened.

sentences.zh: 後有冠雀銜三鱣魚，飛集講堂前，[三]都講取魚進曰：「蛇鱣者，卿大夫服之象也。

translations.literal: Later a stork carried three sturgeon in its beak and alighted before the lecture hall; [three] the lead lecturer took the fish and advanced, saying: 'The serpent-sturgeon is the image of grandees' and ministers' robes.
translations.idiomatic: Then a stork appeared with three sturgeon in its bill and settled before the lecture hall; [3] the chief student picked up the fish and presented them, saying, 'The sturgeon resembles ministers' ceremonial patterns—the insignia of the Three Dukes.'

sentences.zh: 數三者，法三台也。

translations.literal: The number three accords with the Three Terraces.
translations.idiomatic: Three fish mirror the Three Terraces of state.

sentences.zh: 先生自此升矣。

translations.literal: The master from this will ascend.
translations.idiomatic: From this day the master will rise to high office.

sentences.zh: 」年五十，乃始仕州郡。

translations.literal: ' At fifty he first took office in province and commandery.
translations.idiomatic: ' Only at fifty did he begin his career in provincial and local government.

sentences.zh: 注[一]今湖城縣。

translations.literal: [One] Now Hucheng county.
translations.idiomatic: [1] That place is present-day Hucheng county.

sentences.zh: 注[二]續漢*

translations.literal: [Two] Continuation Han*
translations.idiomatic: [2] From the Continuation of the Book of Later Han—

translations.literal: (Monograph)
translations.idiomatic: (treatise)

sentences.zh: **[書]*曰「教授二十餘年，州請召，數稱病不就。

translations.literal: **[Book]* says: 'He taught for over twenty years; the province summoned him repeatedly, but he pleaded illness and did not go.
translations.idiomatic: —the text says: 'He lectured for more than twenty years; the province called him again and again, but he always pleaded illness and refused.'

sentences.zh: 少孤貧，獨與母居，假地種殖，以給供養，諸生嘗有助種藍者，震輒拔，更以距其後，鄉里稱孝」也。

translations.literal: In youth he was orphaned and poor, dwelt alone with his mother, borrowed land to plant and cultivate to supply their needs; students once helped plant indigo—Zhen always pulled it up and replanted farther back; the village praised his filial piety.'"
translations.idiomatic: Orphaned and poor in youth, he lived alone with his mother, rented land to farm for their support. When students tried to help him grow indigo, he uprooted the plants and moved the crop farther off so they could not profit him—the neighbors called him a model of filial devotion.'"

sentences.zh: 注[三]冠音貫，即鸛雀也。

translations.literal: [Three] Guan is read like guan; that is, the stork bird.
translations.idiomatic: [3] 'Capped sparrow' (guan) is read like guan—the stork (guanque).

sentences.zh: 鱣音善。

translations.literal: Zhan is read like shan.
translations.idiomatic: Zhan (sturgeon) is pronounced like shan.

sentences.zh: 韓子云：「鱣似蛇。

translations.literal: Han Feizi says: 'The zhan is like a snake.
translations.idiomatic: Han Feizi says the sturgeon resembles a snake.

sentences.zh: 」臣賢案：續漢及謝承書「鱣」字皆作「釧」，然則「鱣」「釧」古字通也。

translations.literal: ' Your servant Xian notes: in the Continuation Han and Xie Cheng's history the character zhan is always written chuan; thus zhan and chuan were interchangeable in antiquity.
translations.idiomatic: ' Commentator Li Xian observed that in the Continuation Han and Xie Cheng's Later Han the graphs are written chuan instead of zhan—the two forms were equivalent.

sentences.zh: 鱣魚長者不過三尺，黃地黑文，故都講云「蛇釧，卿大夫之服象也」。

translations.literal: The zhan fish grows no longer than three chi, yellow ground with black markings; hence the chief lecturer said 'serpent-chuan is the image of grandees' robes.'
translations.idiomatic: Real sturgeon reach only three chi, yellow with black markings—hence the lecturer's phrase 'snakelike chuan' matched ministers' robe patterns.

sentences.zh: 郭璞云「鱣魚長二三丈，音知然反」，安有鸛雀能勝二三丈乎？

translations.literal: Guo Pu says 'the zhan fish is two or three zhang long, read zhi-ran fan'—how could a stork bird lift two or three zhang?
translations.idiomatic: Guo Pu describes fish two or three zhang long—no stork could lift such quarry.

sentences.zh: 此為鱣明矣。

translations.literal: That this is zhan is clear.
translations.idiomatic: The text must mean the smaller sturgeon, not Guo Pu's giant fish.

sentences.zh: 大將軍鄧騭聞其賢而辟之，舉茂才，四遷荊州刺史﹑東萊太守。

translations.literal: General-in-chief Deng Zhi heard of his worth and recruited him, nominated him as flourishing talent, and in four promotions he became Inspector of Jing province and Governor of Donglai.
translations.idiomatic: The general-in-chief Deng Zhi recruited him on reputation, nominated him as a flourishing talent, and Yang rose through four posts to Inspector of Jing province and Governor of Donglai.

sentences.zh: 當之郡，道經昌邑，[一]故所舉荊州茂才王密為昌邑令，謁見，至夜懷金十斤以遺震。

translations.literal: On his way to the commandery he passed through Changyi; [one] Wang Mi, his former Jingzhou flourishing talent nominee who was now magistrate of Changyi, paid a visit; by night he carried ten jin of gold to present to Zhen.
translations.idiomatic: En route to his post he stopped at Changyi, where Wang Mi—once his nominee as flourishing talent from Jing—was magistrate. Wang came to call and that night tried to gift him ten jin of gold.

sentences.zh: 震曰：「故人知君，君不知故人，何也？

translations.literal: Zhen said: 'The old friend knows you; you do not know the old friend—why?
translations.idiomatic: Zhen replied, 'An old friend ought to know you; evidently you do not know your old friend—why is that?'

sentences.zh: 」密曰：「暮夜無知者。

translations.literal: ' Mi said: 'In the dead of night no one will know.
translations.idiomatic: ' Wang said, 'No one will see us at this hour.'

sentences.zh: 」震曰：「天知，神知，我知，子知。

translations.literal: ' Zhen said: 'Heaven knows, the spirits know, I know, you know.
translations.idiomatic: ' Zhen answered, 'Heaven knows, the spirits know, I know, and you know.'

sentences.zh: 何謂無知！

translations.literal: How call it unknown!
translations.idiomatic: How can you say no one knows?'

sentences.zh: 」密愧而出。

translations.literal: ' Mi ashamed went out.
translations.idiomatic: ' Ashamed, Wang withdrew.

sentences.zh: 後轉涿郡太守。

translations.literal: Later he was transferred to Governor of Zhuo commandery.
translations.idiomatic: He was later transferred to governor of Zhuo commandery.

sentences.zh: 性公廉，不受私謁。

translations.literal: By nature upright and incorrupt, he did not accept private visits.
translations.idiomatic: He was famously honest and refused private solicitations.

sentences.zh: 子孫常蔬食步行，故舊長者或欲令為開產業，震不肯，曰：「使後世稱為清白吏子孫，以此遺之，不亦厚乎！」

translations.literal: His sons and grandsons regularly ate vegetables and went on foot; old friends and elders sometimes wished to have him open estates for them—Zhen refused, saying: 'To leave posterity called descendants of an incorrupt official—what richer legacy than this!'"
translations.idiomatic: His family lived plainly—vegetarian meals and travel on foot. When friends urged him to secure land or wealth for them, he refused: 'If later ages simply call my heirs the children of an honest official, I will have left them enough.'"

sentences.zh: 注[一]昌邑故城在今兗州金鄉縣西北也。

translations.literal: [One] The old city of Changyi is northwest of present Jinxiang county in Yanzhou.
translations.idiomatic: [1] The ruins of Changyi lie northwest of Jinxiang county in Yanzhou.

sentences.zh: 元初四年，征入為太僕，遷太常。

translations.literal: In the fourth year of Yuanchu he was summoned in as Grand Coachman and promoted to Chamberlain for Ceremonials.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanchu 4 he was recalled to court as Grand Coachman and soon promoted to Chamberlain for Ceremonials.

sentences.zh: 先是博士選舉多不以實，震舉薦明經名士陳留楊倫等，[一]顯傳學業，諸儒稱之。

translations.literal: Previously selections for the Erudite post often failed to match merit; Zhen recommended noted scholars who had mastered the classics, such as Yang Lun of Chenliu, [one] bringing learning and instruction into clear view—the scholars praised him.
translations.idiomatic: Doctoral appointments had long been corrupt; Yang nominated distinguished scholars of the classics such as Yang Lun of Chenliu, [1] putting scholarship first—and the scholarly community applauded him.

sentences.zh: 注[一]倫字仲桓。

translations.literal: [One] Lun's courtesy name was Zhonghuan.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Yang Lun's courtesy name was Zhonghuan.

sentences.zh: 謝承書云：「薦楊仲桓等五人，各從家拜博士。」

translations.literal: Xie Cheng's history says: 'He recommended Yang Zhonghuan and five others; each was appointed Erudite from his home.'"
translations.idiomatic: Xie Cheng's Later Han records: 'He put forward Yang Zhonghuan and five others, each of whom received a doctoral appointment without leaving home.'"

sentences.zh: 永寧元年，代劉愷為司徒。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yongning he replaced Liu Kai as Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongning 1 he succeeded Liu Kai as Minister of Education (Situ).

sentences.zh: 明年，鄧太后崩，內寵始橫。

translations.literal: The next year Empress Dowager Deng died, and inner favorites began to run rampant.
translations.idiomatic: The following year Empress Dowager Deng passed away, and palace favorites began to throw their weight around unchecked.

sentences.zh: 安帝乳母王聖，因保養之勤，緣恩放恣；

translations.literal: The wet nurse of Emperor An, Wang Sheng, relying on the diligence of nurture, followed favor into wanton license;
translations.idiomatic: Emperor An's wet nurse Wang Sheng, trading on years of caring for the emperor, parlayed imperial favor into license;

sentences.zh: 聖子女伯榮出入宮掖，傳通姦賂。

translations.literal: Sheng's daughter Borong moved in and out of the palace apartments, transmitting traffic in illicit liaisons and bribes.
translations.idiomatic: her daughter Borong passed freely through the inner palace, brokering intrigue and bribes.

sentences.zh: 震上疏曰：

translations.literal: Zhen submitted a memorial saying:
translations.idiomatic: Yang Zhen presented a memorial:

sentences.zh: 臣聞政以得賢為本，理以去穢為務。

translations.literal: Your subject has heard that government takes obtaining the worthy as its root, and good order takes removing the foul as its task.
translations.idiomatic: I have always understood that good government rests on appointing the worthy, and good order on purging what is base.

sentences.zh: [一]是以唐虞俊乂在官，四凶流放，天上咸服，以致雍熙。

translations.literal: [One] Therefore under Tang and Yu worthy men held office, the Four Fiends were banished; Heaven above submitted alike, achieving harmony and flourishing.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Hence under Yao and Shun worthy men filled office, the Four Fiends were cast out, even Heaven gave its assent, and harmony prevailed.

sentences.zh: [二]方今九德未事，[三]嬖倖充庭。

translations.literal: [Two] Now the Nine Virtues are not yet cultivated; [three] favorites and paramours fill the court.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Today the Nine Virtues go unattended; [3] petty favorites crowd the palace.

sentences.zh: [四]阿母王聖出自賤微，得遭千載，奉養聖躬，雖有推燥居濕之勤，[五]前後賞惠，過報勞苦，而無厭之心，不知紀極，[六]外交屬托，擾亂天下，損辱清朝，塵點日月。

translations.literal: [Four] The nurse Wang Sheng rose from low origins yet encountered an age of fortune and nurtured the sacred person; although she had the toil of laying aside the dry and taking the damp, [five] rewards before and after have exceeded repayment for her labor, yet her heart knows no satiety or limit; [six] outwardly she brokers commissions, throwing the realm into turmoil, besmirching the pure court and casting dust upon sun and moon.
translations.idiomatic: [4] Nurse Wang Sheng began in humble station yet came to nurture the sovereign—a privilege rare in a thousand years. Even counting her sleepless nights beside the crib, [5] every grace she has received already outweighs that labor, yet her greed knows no bounds; [6] outside the palace she peddles influence, unsettles the empire, stains this enlightened reign, and casts a shadow over Your Majesty's brilliance.

sentences.zh: 書誡牝雞牡鳴，[七] 詩刺哲婦喪國。

translations.literal: The Documents warn of hens crowing like cocks; [seven] the Odes rebuke wise wives who ruin states.
translations.idiomatic: The Documents forbid hens that crow like roosters; [7] the Odes lampoon clever women who bring ruin upon a state.

sentences.zh: [八]昔鄭嚴公從母氏之欲，恣驕弟之情，幾至危國，然後加討，春秋貶之，以為失教。

translations.literal: [Eight] Formerly Duke Yan of Zheng followed his mother's desires and indulged his proud younger brother's inclinations, nearly endangering the state before attacking him—the Spring and Autumn Annals censured this as failure to instruct.
translations.idiomatic: [8] Long ago Duke Zhuang of Zheng indulged his mother's whims and his spoiled younger brother until the state nearly collapsed—only then did he strike; the Annals fault him for neglecting proper upbringing.

sentences.zh: [九]夫女子小人，近之喜，遠之怨，實為難養。

translations.literal: [Nine] As for women and petty men—near them they delight; far them they resent—they are truly hard to nurture.
translations.idiomatic: [9] Women and petty men flatter you when indulged and resent you when kept at arm's length—they are the hardest company to manage.

sentences.zh: [一０] 易曰：『無攸遂，在中饋。

translations.literal: [Ten] The Changes say: 'No place to go; in the center, provision of food.'
translations.idiomatic: [10] The Book of Changes says, 'She has no business abroad; her place is the kitchen.'

sentences.zh: 』[一一]言婦人不得與於政事也。

translations.literal: ' [Eleven] This says women may not participate in government affairs.
translations.idiomatic: ' [11] meaning that women must not meddle in state affairs.

sentences.zh: 宜速出阿母，令居外捨，斷絕伯榮，莫使往來，令恩德兩隆，上下俱美。

translations.literal: It is fitting swiftly to expel the nurse, make her dwell in an outer residence, sever Borong and allow no coming and going, so that favor and virtue both flourish and high and low are both beautiful.
translations.idiomatic: Send the wet nurse out of the inner quarters to a house beyond the walls, cut off Borong's access to the palace, and let no further traffic pass between them—so your grace to them and your virtue to the realm may both stand complete.

sentences.zh: 惟陛下絕婉孌之私，割不忍之心，[一二]留神萬機，誡慎拜爵，減省獻御，損節征發。

translations.literal: Only may Your Majesty sever private doting, cut off the heart of unwillingness, [twelve] apply mind to the myriad handles of state, be cautious in investing titles, reduce and cut tribute to the inner court, and curtail conscription and levies.
translations.idiomatic: I beg you to set aside private affection, harden your heart where partiality tempts you, [12] give the myriad affairs of state your full attention, ennoble only the deserving, scale back what is lavished on the harem, and ease taxes and corvée.

sentences.zh: 令野無鶴鳴之歎，[一三]朝無小明之悔，[一四]大東不興於今，[一五]勞止不怨於下。

translations.literal: Make the wilds have no sighs of the crane's cry, [thirteen] the court no regrets of 'Little Bright,' [fourteen] the great east not rise in the present, [fifteen] the weary not resent below.
translations.idiomatic: Then the countryside will not echo the laments of the 'Crane Cry,' [13] the morning court will not relive the remorse of 'Small Bright,' [14] the torments sung in 'Great East' will find no echo today, [15] and common folk who toil will harbor no grievance.

sentences.zh: [一六] 擬蹤往古，比德哲王，豈不休哉！

translations.literal: [Sixteen] Matching tracks with antiquity, comparing virtue with sage kings—how could it not be splendid!
translations.idiomatic: [16] Walk in the footsteps of ancient sage-kings and align your virtue with theirs—could any ruler wish for a finer achievement?

sentences.zh: 奏御，帝以示阿母等，內幸皆懷忿恚。

translations.literal: When the memorial was presented, the emperor showed it to the wet nurse and her circle; the inner favorites all nursed anger and resentment.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor handed the memorial to the wet nurse and her allies; the palace favorites boiled with resentment.

sentences.zh: 而伯榮驕淫尤甚，與故朝陽侯劉護從兄纓交通，[一七]纓遂以為妻，得襲護爵，位至侍中。

translations.literal: But Borong's arrogance and licentiousness were extreme; she associated with Liu Hu's elder cousin Ying by marriage. [Seventeen] Ying then took her as wife, succeeded to Hu's marquisate, and rose to Palace Attendant.
translations.idiomatic: Borong grew ever more brazen and dissolute; she took up with Ying, the former Marquis of Chaoyang Liu Hu's cousin. [17] Ying married her, inherited Hu's title, and climbed to Palace Attendant.

sentences.zh: 震深疾之，復詣闕上疏曰：

translations.literal: Zhen deeply hated this and again went to the palace gate to submit a memorial saying:
translations.idiomatic: Yang Zhen found this intolerable and went again to the palace with another memorial:

sentences.zh: 臣聞高祖與腢臣約，非功臣不得封，故經制父死子繼，兄亡弟及，以防篡也。

translations.literal: Your subject has heard that Gaozu made a pact with his ministers: unless they were merit officials they might not be enfeoffed; hence the classic norm—father dies, son succeeds; elder brother dies, younger brother follows—to guard against usurpation.
translations.idiomatic: I understand that Han Gaozu pledged his ministers that only men of proven merit would receive fiefs—hence the rule of father-to-son and brother-to-brother succession, meant to block usurpation.

sentences.zh: [一八]伏見詔書封故朝陽侯劉護再從兄纓襲護爵為侯。

translations.literal: [Eighteen] I have seen with bowed head an edict enfeoffing Ying, Liu Hu's second cousin once removed, to inherit Hu's marquisate as Marquis.
translations.idiomatic: [18] I have read an edict making Ying—Liu Hu's second cousin—marquis in Hu's place.

sentences.zh: 護同產弟威，今猶見在。

translations.literal: Hu's full younger brother Wei is still alive today.
translations.idiomatic: Yet Liu Hu's own brother Wei is still living.

sentences.zh: 臣聞天子專封封有功，諸侯專爵爵有德。

translations.literal: Your subject has heard that the Son of Heaven alone enfeoffs those with merit, and marquises alone invest those with virtue.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven awards fiefs only for merit; nobles ennoble only the deserving.

sentences.zh: 今纓無佗功行，但以配阿母女，一時之閒，既位侍中，又至封侯，不稽舊制，不合經義，行人諠嘩，百姓不安。

translations.literal: Now Ying has no other merit or conduct—only that he married the wet nurse's daughter; in a single interval he has both attained Palace Attendant and reached marquis enfeoffment, not examining old regulations, not matching classical meaning; travelers clamor, the hundred surnames are uneasy.
translations.idiomatic: Ying has achieved nothing beyond marrying the wet nurse's daughter; overnight he holds Palace Attendant and a marquisate—contrary to precedent and the classics. Travelers gossip; the people are unsettled.

sentences.zh: 陛下宜覽鏡既往，順帝之則。

translations.literal: Your Majesty ought to view the mirror of what has passed and accord with the norms of the emperors.
translations.idiomatic: Your Majesty should take the past as a mirror and follow the pattern set by your imperial predecessors.

sentences.zh: 書奏不省。

translations.literal: The memorial was submitted and not examined.
translations.idiomatic: The throne ignored his plea.

sentences.zh: 注[一]墨子曰：「夫尚賢者，政本也。

translations.literal: [One] Mozi says: 'To esteem the worthy is the root of government.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Mozi states: 'Elevating the worthy is the foundation of rule.'

sentences.zh: 」左傳曰：「為國者，如農夫之務去草焉。」

translations.literal: ' The Zuo Commentary says: 'One who governs a state is like a farmer bent on rooting out weeds.'"
translations.idiomatic: ' The Zuo Commentary adds: 'Governing a realm is like farming—you must tear out the weeds.'"

sentences.zh: 注[二]尚書曰：「四罪而天下咸服。

translations.literal: [Two] The Documents say: 'Four crimes punished and all under Heaven submitted alike.
translations.idiomatic: [2] The Book of Documents: 'Four criminals punished—and the realm submitted.'

sentences.zh: 」又曰：「黎人於變時雍，庶績咸熙。

translations.literal: ' It also says: 'The black-haired people were transformed in timely harmony; all tasks alike flourished.
translations.idiomatic: ' It also reads: 'The people were transformed in harmonious season; every duty prospered.'

sentences.zh: 」雍，和也。

translations.literal: ' Yong means harmony.
translations.idiomatic: ' Yong here means harmony.

sentences.zh: 熙，廣也。

translations.literal: Xi means broad.
translations.idiomatic: Xi means flourishing or widespread.

sentences.zh: 注[三]尚書戲繇謨曰：「亦行有九德：寬而栗，柔而立，願而龔，亂而敬，擾而毅，直而溫，簡而廉，剛而塞，強而誼。

translations.literal: [Three] The Documents, Counsels of Gao Yao, says: 'Also conduct has nine virtues: broad yet stern (kuan er li), gentle yet standing firm (rou er li), humble yet reverent (yuan er gong), orderly yet respectful (luan er jing), teachable yet resolute (rao er yi), straight yet mild (zhi er wen), economical yet incorrupt (jian er lian), firm yet substantial (gang er se), strong yet principled (qiang er yi).
translations.idiomatic: [3] The Counsels of Gao Yao in the Book of Documents enumerates the nine virtues—generosity balanced with discipline, suppleness with backbone, and seven further pairings that define fit characters for office.'

sentences.zh: 」又曰：「九德咸事，俊乂在官。」

translations.literal: ' It also says: 'When the nine virtues all find employment, outstanding men hold office.'"
translations.idiomatic: ' The text continues: 'When those virtues are all at work, the worthy fill the government.'"

sentences.zh: 注[四]謚法曰：「賤而得愛曰嬖。」

translations.literal: [Four] The Posthumous-title canon says: 'Low yet beloved is called bi (favorite).'
translations.idiomatic: [4] Canon of posthumous names: 'One who is base yet favored is called a pet (bi).'

sentences.zh: 注[五]孝經援神契曰「母之於子也，鞠養殷勤，推燥居濕，絕少分甘」也。

translations.literal: [Five] The Xiaojing Yuanshen Qi says: 'As for the mother toward the child, nurture is diligent; she pushes aside the dry and takes the damp, denies herself the sweet and shares it.'"
translations.idiomatic: [5] The Xiaojing Yuanshen Qi describes a mother's care—she takes the damp bed and gives the dry, saves the savory bit for her child.'"

sentences.zh: 注[六]左傳曰，縉雲氏有不材子，聚斂積實，不知紀極。

translations.literal: [Six] The Zuo Commentary says: the Jinyun clan had an unworthy son who hoarded amassed wealth and knew no limit or bound.
translations.idiomatic: [6] The Zuo Commentary tells of a worthless son of the Jinyun clan who piled up riches without satiety.

sentences.zh: 注[七]牝，雌也。

translations.literal: [Seven] Pin means female.
translations.idiomatic: [7] Pin denotes the female.

sentences.zh: 牡，雄也。

translations.literal: Mu means male.
translations.idiomatic: Mu denotes the male.

sentences.zh: 尚書：「古人有言，牝雞無晨，牝雞之晨，唯家之索。」

translations.literal: The Documents: 'The ancients had a saying: the hen has no morning; the hen's morning—only the household's exhaustion.'"
translations.idiomatic: The Documents warn: 'As the ancients said, let no hen announce the dawn—if she crows, the household is doomed.'"

sentences.zh: 注[八]詩大雅曰：「哲夫成城，哲婦傾城。」

translations.literal: [Eight] The Great Odes say: 'A wise husband builds the wall; a wise wife overturns the wall.'"
translations.idiomatic: [8] The 'Sheng Min' ode says: 'A wise husband builds the wall; a wise wife overturns it.'"

sentences.zh: 注[九]嚴公，莊公也，避明帝諱改焉。

translations.literal: [Nine] Duke Yan is Duke Zhuang—changed to avoid Emperor Ming's taboo.
translations.idiomatic: [9] 'Duke Yan' stands for Duke Zhuang of Zheng, renamed to honor Emperor Ming's taboo.

sentences.zh: 左傳，鄭莊公殺母弟段，稱鄭伯，譏失教也。

translations.literal: In the Zuo Commentary, Duke Zhuang of Zheng killed his younger uterine brother Duan; he is titled Earl of Zheng—this censures failure to instruct.
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Commentary records Duke Zhuang executing his brother yet styles him merely 'Earl of Zheng'—a rebuke for failed upbringing.

sentences.zh: 注[一０]論語曰「唯女子與小人為難養，近之則不遜，遠之則怨」也。

translations.literal: [Ten] The Analects says: 'Only women and petty men are hard to nurture—near them they are insolent; far them they resent.'"
translations.idiomatic: [10] The Analects: 'Women and petty men are hardest to serve—intimate with them and they grow rude; keep distance and they turn bitter.'"

sentences.zh: 注[一一]家人卦六二爻辭也。

translations.literal: [Eleven] This is the line text of the Family hexagram, six in the second place.
translations.idiomatic: [11] That line belongs to the second yin of the Family hexagram.

sentences.zh: 鄭玄注曰：「二為陰爻，得正於內；

translations.literal: Zheng Xuan's commentary says: 'The second is a yin line, obtaining correctness inward;
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan explains: 'The second line is yin and holds the correct position within;'

sentences.zh: 五，陽爻也，得正於外。

translations.literal: the fifth is a yang line, obtaining correctness outward.
translations.idiomatic: the fifth is yang and holds the correct position without.'"

sentences.zh: 猶婦人自修正於內，丈夫修正於外。

translations.literal: It is as the wife corrects herself inwardly, the husband corrects himself outwardly.
translations.idiomatic: Just as the wife orders her conduct within the household, the husband orders his without.

sentences.zh: 無攸遂，言婦人無敢自遂也。

translations.literal: 'No place to go' means the wife dares not follow her own whim.
translations.idiomatic: 'No venture succeeds abroad' means a wife must not pursue her private will.

sentences.zh: 爻體離，又互體坎，火位在下，水在上，□之象也。

translations.literal: The line's body is Li; its mutual body is Kan; fire sits below, water above—the image of □.
translations.idiomatic: The hexagram shows Li with an inner Kan—fire below, water above—the figure of [missing graph in source].

sentences.zh: 饋，食也，故云在中饋也。」

translations.literal: Kui is food; hence it is said 'in the center, provision of food.'"
translations.idiomatic: Kui means 'to feed,' which is why the line speaks of keeping to the kitchen.'"

sentences.zh: 注[一二]詩國風候人篇序曰：「曹共公遠君子而近小人。

translations.literal: [Twelve] The preface to 'The Guards' in the Airs of the States says: 'Duke Gong of Cao kept worthy men at distance and drew petty men near.
translations.idiomatic: [12] The preface to 'The Guards' says Duke Gong of Cao favored petty men and spurned gentlemen.

sentences.zh: 」其詩曰：「婉兮孌兮，季女斯饑。

translations.literal: ' Its poem says: 'graceful, lovely—the youngest daughter thus hungers.
translations.idiomatic: ' The poem runs: 'Delicate, lovely—the young bride starves.'

sentences.zh: 」婉，少貌。

translations.literal: ' Wan means youthful appearance.
translations.idiomatic: ' Wan describes youthful beauty.

sentences.zh: 孌，好貌也。

translations.literal: Luan means lovely appearance.
translations.idiomatic: Luan describes comeliness.

sentences.zh: 注[一三]詩小雅序曰：「鶴鳴，誨宣王也。

translations.literal: [Thirteen] The Lesser Ya preface says: 'Crane Cry instructs King Xuan.
translations.idiomatic: [13] The Lesser Ya preface: 'Crane Cry' was meant to instruct King Xuan.

sentences.zh: 」鄭玄注云：「教周宣王求賢人之未仕者。

translations.literal: ' Zheng Xuan notes: 'It teaches King Xuan of Zhou to seek worthies not yet in office.
translations.idiomatic: ' Zheng Xuan explains that it urges King Xuan to recruit talent still outside government.

sentences.zh: 」其詩曰：「鶴鳴於九戲，聲聞於野。

translations.literal: ' Its poem says: 'The crane cries in the nine marshes; its voice is heard in the wilds.
translations.idiomatic: ' The ode says: 'The crane calls in the nine marsh shallows; its cry carries across the moor.' (The received text uses an unusual graph where the standard Ode has 'nine river-bends,' i.e. the nine marshy pools.)

sentences.zh: 」言身隱而名著，喻賢者雖隱居，人咸知之。

translations.literal: ' This speaks of the body hidden yet fame manifest—it likens how worthies, though in reclusion, are all known.
translations.idiomatic: ' It praises hermits whose reputations outshine their hiding places.

sentences.zh: 注[一四]詩小雅序曰：「小明，大夫悔仕於亂也。

translations.literal: [Fourteen] The Lesser Ya preface says: 'Little Bright—a grandee regrets serving in disorder.
translations.idiomatic: [14] The Lesser Ya preface: 'Little Bright' voices a minister's regret at serving a chaotic court.

sentences.zh: 」小明者，言周幽王日小其明，損其政事，以至於亂。

translations.literal: ' 'Little Bright' means King You of Zhou daily diminished his brightness and harmed government affairs, reaching disorder.
translations.idiomatic: ' The title means King You daily dimmed his discernment and ruined governance until chaos followed.

sentences.zh: 注[一五]詩小雅序：「大東，刺亂也。

translations.literal: [Fifteen] The Lesser Ya preface: 'Great East rebukes disorder.
translations.idiomatic: [15] The Lesser Ya preface calls 'Great East' a satire on turmoil.

sentences.zh: 」其詩曰：「小東大東，杼柚其空。

translations.literal: ' Its poem says: 'Little east, great east—the shuttles and beams stand empty.
translations.idiomatic: ' It opens: 'East near and east far—the looms are stripped bare.'

sentences.zh: 」鄭玄注云：「小亦於東，大亦於東，言賦斂多也。」

translations.literal: ' Zheng Xuan notes: 'Small also in the east, large also in the east—this speaks of heavy levies.'"
translations.idiomatic: ' Zheng Xuan glosses: both regions lie east of Zhou—the line protests crushing taxes.'"

sentences.zh: 注[一六]詩大雅序曰：「人勞，刺厲王也。

translations.literal: [Sixteen] The Greater Ya preface says: 'The People Are Weary rebukes King Li.
translations.idiomatic: [16] The Greater Ya preface treats 'The People Are Weary' as satire of King Li.

sentences.zh: 」其詩曰「人亦勞止，迄可小康」也。

translations.literal: ' Its poem says: 'The people are weary indeed—perhaps we may have a little rest.'"
translations.idiomatic: ' The verse asks: 'The people are worn out—may we yet taste a little ease?'"

sentences.zh: 注[一七]護，泗水王歙之從曾孫。

translations.literal: [Seventeen] Hu was the second cousin once removed of Xi, King of Sishui.
translations.idiomatic: [17] Liu Hu was a collateral descendant of Xi, the king of Sishui.

sentences.zh: 注[一八]公羊傳曰：「劉子﹑單子以王猛入於王城者何？

translations.literal: [Eighteen] The Gongyang Commentary says: 'Liu and Shan brought Prince Meng into the royal city—what of it?
translations.idiomatic: [18] The Gongyang asks why Liu and Shan led Prince Meng into the royal city—

sentences.zh: 西周也。

translations.literal: Western Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: because it was Western Zhou territory.

sentences.zh: 其言入何？

translations.literal: Why does it say enter?
translations.idiomatic: Why does the text use the word 'enter'?

sentences.zh: 篡辭也。

translations.literal: The language of usurpation.
translations.idiomatic: The phrasing insinuates usurpation.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，王子猛卒。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, Prince Meng died.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter Prince Meng died.

sentences.zh: 此未踰年之君，其稱王子猛卒何？

translations.literal: This ruler has not passed a year—why is he called 'Prince Meng died'?
translations.idiomatic: He had not yet completed a year of rule—why is he still called 'Prince' at death?

sentences.zh: 不予當也。

translations.literal: He is not granted as proper.
translations.idiomatic: The text denies him full royal standing.

sentences.zh: 不予當者，不與當父死子繼，兄亡弟及也。」

translations.literal: Not granting as proper means not granting the proper father-dies-son-succeeds, elder-brother-dies-younger-brother-follows.'"
translations.idiomatic: To withhold 'proper' is to refuse the regular succession of father to son or brother to brother.'"

sentences.zh: 延光二年，代劉愷為太尉。

translations.literal: In the second year of Yanguang he replaced Liu Kai as Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In Yanguang 2 he succeeded Liu Kai as Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 帝舅大鴻臚耿寶薦中常侍李閏兄於震，震不從。

translations.literal: The emperor's uncle, Grand Herald Geng Bao, recommended the elder brother of Palace Attendant Li Run to Zhen; Zhen did not comply.
translations.idiomatic: The imperial uncle Geng Bao, Grand Herald, asked Yang to take the older brother of the eunuch Li Run; Yang refused.

sentences.zh: 寶乃自往候震曰：「李常侍國家所重，欲令公辟其兄，寶唯傳上意耳。

translations.literal: Bao then went in person to visit Zhen, saying: 'Attendant Li is one the state esteems; I wish the duke to recruit his elder brother—I Bao only convey the superiors' intent.
translations.idiomatic: Geng called on Yang in person: 'Attendant Li is a pillar of the court. The court wants you to give his brother a post—I am only the messenger.'

sentences.zh: 」[一]震曰：「如朝廷欲令三府辟召，故宜有尚書□。

translations.literal: ' [One] Zhen said: 'If the court wishes the Three Offices to issue recruitment, there should therefore be a [graph missing] from the Masters of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: ' [1] Yang replied, 'If the court means the Three Excellencies to appoint him, the edict should come from the Masters of Writing [text damaged].'

sentences.zh: 」遂拒不許，寶大恨而去。

translations.literal: ' He thereupon refused and would not allow; Bao greatly hated and left.
translations.idiomatic: ' He flatly declined. Geng left in a fury.

sentences.zh: 皇后兄執金吾閻顯亦薦所親厚於震，震又不從。

translations.literal: The empress's brother, Bearer of the Mace Yan Xian, also recommended his intimate friends to Zhen; Zhen again did not comply.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Xian, the empress's brother and Bearer of the Mace, pushed his own cronies; Yang again refused.

sentences.zh: 司空劉授聞之，[二]即辟此二人，旬日中皆見拔擢。

translations.literal: Minister of Works Liu Shou heard of it, [two] and immediately recruited these two; within ten days both were seen promoted.
translations.idiomatic: Minister of Works Liu Shou, [2] hearing of it, took both men on; within days they were promoted.

sentences.zh: 由是震益見怨。

translations.literal: From this Zhen was all the more resented.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Zhen made new enemies.

sentences.zh: 注[一]言非己本心，傳在上之意。

translations.literal: [One] It says it was not his own mind—he transmitted the superiors' intent.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Geng disclaimed personal motive—he was only passing down the court's will.

sentences.zh: 注[二]漢官儀：「授字孟春，武原人。」

translations.literal: [Two] Han Guan Yi: 'Shou, courtesy Mengchun, a man of Wuyuan.'"
translations.idiomatic: [2] The Han Guan Yi gives Liu Shou, style Mengchun, as a native of Wuyuan.'"

sentences.zh: 時詔遣使者大為阿母修第，中常侍樊豐及侍中周廣﹑謝惲等更相扇動，傾搖朝廷。

translations.literal: At the time an edict sent envoys to greatly build the wet nurse a mansion; Palace Attendant Fan Feng and Palace Attendants Zhou Guang, Xie Yun, and others in turn fanned and stirred, toppling the court.
translations.idiomatic: An edict commissioned a grand residence for the wet nurse. Fan Feng, Zhou Guang, Xie Yun, and their circle fanned one another on and began to rock the government.

sentences.zh: 震復上疏曰：

translations.literal: Zhen again submitted a memorial saying:
translations.idiomatic: Yang Zhen sent up another memorial:

sentences.zh: 臣聞古者九年耕必有三年之儲，故堯遭洪水，人無菜色。

translations.literal: Your subject has heard that in antiquity nine years' ploughing must yield three years' store; hence when Yao suffered the great flood, the people had no green-faced hunger.
translations.idiomatic: I have read that ancient policy required three years' grain in store for every nine farmed; that is why even Yao's flood years did not leave the people gaunt with hunger.

sentences.zh: [一]臣伏念方今□害發起，彌彌滋甚，[二]百姓空虛，不能自贍。

translations.literal: [One] Your subject humbly reflects that now □ harm arises and spreads ever wider; [two] the hundred surnames are emptied within and cannot support themselves.
translations.idiomatic: [1] I see disaster sprouting on every side, each day more virulent; [2] the common people are destitute and cannot keep themselves.

sentences.zh: 重以螟蝗，羌虜鈔掠，三邊震擾，戰□之役至今未息，兵甲軍糧不能復給。

translations.literal: Added to this are caterpillar and locust plagues, Qiang raiders' pillage, the three frontiers shaken and troubled, war □ campaigns not yet stilled to this day—arms and army grain cannot again be supplied.
translations.idiomatic: Add caterpillars, locusts, Qiang raids, and strife on every frontier—campaigns [text damaged] still drain the treasury, and we can no longer arm the troops or fill the granaries.

sentences.zh: 大司農帑藏匱乏，殆非社稷安寧之時。

translations.literal: The Grand Minister of Agriculture's treasuries are exhausted—hardly a time for the altars of soil and grain to be secure.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Minister of Agriculture's coffers are empty—this is no season to pretend the altars are safe.

sentences.zh: 伏見詔書為阿母興起津城門內第捨，[三]合兩為一，連裡竟街，[四] 雕修繕飾，窮極巧伎。

translations.literal: I have seen with bowed head an edict to raise the wet nurse a residence inside the Jin-cheng gate, [three] joining two into one, linking wards across the whole street, [four] carved repair and adornment, exhausting clever craft.
translations.idiomatic: Yet I read an edict to build the wet nurse a palace inside the Jin-cheng gate, [3] merging two wards into one compound running the length of the avenue, [4] carved, painted, and furnished with every artisan's trick.

sentences.zh: 今盛夏土王，而攻山採石，其大匠左校別部將作合數十處，[五]轉相迫促，為費巨億。

translations.literal: Now in high summer earth rules the season, yet they attack mountains and quarry stone; the Grand Artisan's Left School and separate departments of the Director of Works total several tens of sites, [five] pressing one another in turn, costing vast billions.
translations.idiomatic: It is midsummer, the season when earth predominates, yet laborers tear open hills for stone. The Director of Works and the Left School split crews across dozens of sites, [5] harrying one another at ruinous cost—billions in coin.

sentences.zh: 周廣﹑謝惲兄弟，與國無肺腑枝葉之屬，依倚近幸奸佞之人，與樊豐﹑王永等分威共權，屬托州郡，傾動大臣。

translations.literal: Zhou Guang and Xie Yun's brothers have with the state no chest-and-belly branch-leaf kinship; they rely on near-favored crafty flatterers, share authority and power with Fan Feng, Wang Yong, and others, commission province and commandery, and overturn great ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Guang and Xie Yun are no kin of the imperial house; they cling to palace favorites, split power with Fan Feng and Wang Yong, pull strings in every province, and sway the highest ministers.

sentences.zh: 宰司辟召，承望旨意，招來海內貪污之人，受其貨賂，至有臧錮棄世之徒復得顯用。

translations.literal: High ministers recruit on cue to imperial hints, summoning within the seas greedy corrupt men, accepting their goods and bribes—even men with bribery stigma cast from the world again attain conspicuous employment.
translations.idiomatic: Ministers appoint whoever the favorites hint at, taking bribes to reinstate embezzlers and men once banned from office.

sentences.zh: [六] 白黑溷淆，清濁同源，天下讙嘩，咸曰財貨上流，為朝結譏。

translations.literal: [Six] White and black are muddled together; clear and turbid share one source; all under Heaven clamors, all saying wealth floats upward, weaving ridicule for the court.
translations.idiomatic: [6] Right and wrong blur; worthy and base mingle. The realm mutters that silver flows upward and the court becomes a laughingstock.

sentences.zh: 臣聞師言：『上之所取，財盡則怨，力盡則叛。

translations.literal: Your subject has heard the masters say: 'What superiors take—when wealth is exhausted there is resentment; when strength is exhausted there is rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: The masters teach: 'When a ruler squeezes his people dry of wealth they resent him; when he exhausts their strength they rebel.'

sentences.zh: 』怨叛之人，不可復使，故曰：『百姓不足，君誰與足？

translations.literal: ' Men resentful and rebellious cannot again be employed; hence it is said: 'When the hundred surnames are insufficient, with whom is the lord sufficient?
translations.idiomatic: ' A resentful people cannot be governed. Hence the saying: 'If the people lack enough, what can sustain their ruler?'

sentences.zh: 』[七]惟陛下度之。

translations.literal: ' [Seven] Only may Your Majesty measure this.
translations.idiomatic: ' [7] I beg Your Majesty to weigh these words.'

sentences.zh: 豐﹑惲等見震連切諫不從，無所顧忌，遂詐作詔書，調發司農錢谷﹑大匠見徒材木，各起家捨﹑園池﹑廬觀，役費無數。

translations.literal: Feng, Yun, and others saw Zhen's successive stern remonstrance unheeded and had no scruples; they then forged edicts, transferring out Grand Minister of Agriculture cash and grain, Director of Works present convicts' timber, each raising house dwellings, gardens and pools, lodges and outlooks—labor costs without number.
translations.idiomatic: Seeing Yang's repeated warnings ignored, Fan Feng and Xie Yun grew brazen: they forged orders, drew grain and coin from the Grand Minister of Agriculture, stripped timber from convict labor under the Director of Works, and threw up mansions, parks, and towers at limitless expense.

sentences.zh: 注[一]言有儲蓄，人無食菜之饑色也。

translations.literal: [One] It speaks of having stores so people lack vegetable-eating hunger hue.
translations.idiomatic: [1] i.e., granaries were full and no one showed famine's greenish pallor.

sentences.zh: 注[二]彌彌猶稍稍也。

translations.literal: [Two] Mimi is like shaoshao (gradually).
translations.idiomatic: [2] Mimi means 'little by little.'

sentences.zh: 韋孟詩曰「彌彌其失」也。

translations.literal: Wei Meng's poem says 'mimi its failures.'"
translations.idiomatic: Wei Meng wrote of faults spreading 'mimi'—ever wider.'"

sentences.zh: 注[三]津城門，洛陽南面西頭門也。

translations.literal: [Three] Jin-cheng gate is the westernmost gate on Luoyang's south face.
translations.idiomatic: [3] The Jin-cheng gate is the southwestern gate on Luoyang's south wall.

sentences.zh: 注[四]合兩坊而為一宅。

translations.literal: [Four] Joining two wards into one residence.
translations.idiomatic: [4] Two residential wards were merged into one compound.

sentences.zh: 裡即坊也。

translations.literal: Li is the same as ward.
translations.idiomatic: Li here means a residential ward (fang).

sentences.zh: 注[五]續漢志將作大匠，秩二千石。

translations.literal: [Five] Continuation Han Treatise: Director of Works, rank two thousand shi.
translations.idiomatic: [5] Per the Later Han Treatise, the Director of Works ranked two thousand shi.

sentences.zh: 左校令，秩六百石。

translations.literal: Left School director, rank six hundred shi.
translations.idiomatic: The Left School director ranked six hundred shi.

sentences.zh: 注[六]有臧賄禁錮之人也。

translations.literal: [Six] Men with bribery stigma and prohibition bonds.
translations.idiomatic: [6] Men convicted of corruption and barred from office.

sentences.zh: 注[七]論語有若對魯哀公之詞。

translations.literal: [Seven] The Analects—you Ruo's reply to Duke Ai of Lu.
translations.idiomatic: [7] You Ruo's answer to Duke Ai of Lu in the Analects.

sentences.zh: 震因地震，復上疏曰：

translations.literal: Because of an earthquake, Zhen again submitted a memorial saying:
translations.idiomatic: After an earthquake struck, Yang Zhen submitted yet another memorial:

sentences.zh: 臣蒙恩備台輔，不能奉宣政化，調和陰陽，去年十*

translations.literal: Your subject has received favor to fill the terrace assistants, yet cannot propagate governance and transform, harmonize yin and yang; last year's tenth *
translations.idiomatic: I hold a seat among the Three Ducal Ministers yet fail to harmonize yin and yang or spread good rule—last year's tenth [month—

translations.literal: (One)
translations.idiomatic: (damage marker (一))

sentences.zh: *[二]月四日，京師地動。

translations.literal: *[Two] month, fourth day, the capital earth moved.
translations.idiomatic: [2] —on the fourth day of that month the capital was shaken by an earthquake.

sentences.zh: 臣聞師言：『地者陰精，當安靜承陽。

translations.literal: Your subject has heard masters say: 'Earth is yin's essence; it ought quietly to bear yang.
translations.idiomatic: The masters say earth embodies yin and should rest quiet beneath yang.'

sentences.zh: 』而今動搖者，陰道盛也。

translations.literal: ' Now that it shakes and stirs—yin's way is excessive.
translations.idiomatic: ' When it trembles, yin has grown too strong.'

sentences.zh: 其日戊辰，三者皆土，位在中宮，[一]此中臣近官盛於持權用事之象也。

translations.literal: That day was wuchen; the three are all earth, positioned in the central palace—[one] this is the image of inner ministers and near officials flourishing in grasping power and handling affairs.
translations.idiomatic: The day was wuchen—stem and branch both earth, seated in the central palace—[1] an omen that palace insiders clutch power.

sentences.zh: 臣伏惟陛下以邊境未寧，躬自菲薄，宮殿垣屋傾倚，枝柱而已，[二]無所興造，欲令遠近咸知政化之清流，商邑之翼翼也。

translations.literal: Your subject humbly reflects that Your Majesty, because the borders are not tranquil, personally practices austerity; palace halls' walls and roofs lean—props and pillars only, [two] nothing raised or built—wishing to make far and near all know the clear stream of governance and transformation, Shang capital's awe.
translations.idiomatic: You have tightened the belt because the frontiers smolder—palaces lean on props with no new building, [2] hoping the realm will see your reign as pure as the Shang hymn's well-ordered capital.

sentences.zh: [三]而親近幸臣，未崇斷金，[四] 驕溢踰法，多請徒士，盛修第捨，賣弄威福。

translations.literal: [Three] Yet those near and favored ministers have not esteemed severing metal; [four] arrogance overflows beyond law, they often request convict laborers, grandly repair mansions, peddle awe and blessing.
translations.idiomatic: [3] Yet your favorites share no 'metal-severing' loyalty; [4] they swagger beyond the law, requisition convicts, raise lavish mansions, and traffic in borrowed majesty.

sentences.zh: 道路讙嘩，觿所聞見。

translations.literal: Roads clamor—the ivory tally youth hear and see.
translations.idiomatic: Every street buzzes with gossip—even common folk see and hear it.

sentences.zh: 地動之變，近在城郭，殆為此發。

translations.literal: The earth-quake's change is near within walls and moats—probably this arose from it.
translations.idiomatic: The quake struck at the capital's walls—it surely answers such misconduct.

sentences.zh: 又冬無宿雪，春節未雨，百僚燋心，而繕修不止，誠致旱之征也。

translations.literal: Moreover winter had no lingering snow, spring season had no rain—the hundred officials' hearts are scorched, yet repair does not cease—truly a sign bringing drought.
translations.idiomatic: Winter brought no lasting snow; spring no timely rain. Officials fret while construction never stops—a recipe for drought.

sentences.zh: 書曰：『僭恆陽若，臣無作威作福玉食。

translations.literal: The Documents say: 'Presumption constant yang complies; ministers—do not make awe, make blessing, jade food.
translations.idiomatic: The Documents warn: 'When rulers grow arrogant, drought-like yang follows; ministers must not play lord or feast like kings.'

sentences.zh: 』[五]唯陛下奮干剛之德，[六]棄驕奢之臣，以掩訞言之口，奉承皇天之戒，無令威福久移於下。

translations.literal: ' [Five] Only may Your Majesty rouse Qian's firm virtue, [six] cast off arrogant extravagant ministers, to cover deceitful speech's mouth, receive and uphold August Heaven's warning—do not let awe and blessing long shift below.
translations.idiomatic: ' [5] Rouse the firm virtue of Qian; [6] banish arrogant ministers; silence slander; heed Heaven's warning—do not let power and favor linger long below the throne.'

sentences.zh: 注[一]戊干辰支皆土也，並地動，故言三者。

translations.literal: [One] Wu stem and chen branch are both earth; together with earth-quake, hence it says three.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Wu (stem) and chen (branch) are both earth elements—together with the quake they make 'three' earth signs.

sentences.zh: 注[二]倚，邪也。

translations.literal: [Two] Yi means leaning/slant.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Yi means tilting—here, out of true.

sentences.zh: 注音竹主反。

translations.literal: Commentary sound zhu-zhu fan.
translations.idiomatic: The gloss gives the fanqie spelling zhu-zhu.

sentences.zh: 注[三]詩商頌「商邑翼翼，四方之極」也。

translations.literal: [Three] The Shang hymn 'Shang capital awe-inspiring—polestar of the four quarters.'"
translations.idiomatic: [3] From the Shang hymn: 'How majestic the royal capital—model to the four quarters.'"

sentences.zh: 注[四]易系辭曰：「二人同心，其利斷金。

translations.literal: [Four] The Changes Appended Texts say: 'Two men share one heart—their keenness severs metal.
translations.idiomatic: [4] The Appended Texts: 'Two minds as one can cut bronze.'

sentences.zh: 」言邪佞之臣，不與上同心。

translations.literal: ' It speaks of crafty flattering ministers not sharing one heart with superiors.
translations.idiomatic: ' Flatterers do not share the ruler's purpose.'

sentences.zh: 注[五]尚書洪範之詞也。

translations.literal: [Five] These are words from the Documents' Great Plan.
translations.idiomatic: [5] Quoted from the 'Great Plan' in the Book of Documents.

sentences.zh: 僭，差也。

translations.literal: Jian means excess/error.
translations.idiomatic: Jian means transgression or departure from due measure.

sentences.zh: 若，順也。

translations.literal: Ruo means comply.
translations.idiomatic: Ruo means 'to accord with.'

sentences.zh: 君行僭差，則常陽順之也。

translations.literal: When the lord's conduct is presumptuous excess, then constant yang complies with it.
translations.idiomatic: When the ruler oversteps, relentless drought-like yang follows.

sentences.zh: 言唯君得專威福，為美食。

translations.literal: It says only the lord may monopolize awe and blessing, making fine food.
translations.idiomatic: Only the sovereign may wield blessing and terror and feast as supreme.

sentences.zh: 注[六]易曰：「大哉干乎！

translations.literal: [Six] The Changes say: 'How great is Qian!
translations.idiomatic: [6] The Changes exclaim: 'Great is Qian!'

sentences.zh: 剛健中正，純粹精也。」

translations.literal: Firm, healthy, central, correct—pure, unmixed, essential.'"
translations.idiomatic: Firm, central, true—pure spirit incarnate.'"

sentences.zh: 震前後所上，轉有切至，帝既不平之，而樊豐等皆側目憤怨，俱以其名儒，未敢加害。

translations.literal: What Zhen submitted before and after became increasingly cutting; the emperor was already displeased, while Fan Feng and others all glanced sideways in rage and resentment—all because he was a famed Ru, not daring to harm him.
translations.idiomatic: Yang's memorials grew sharper. The emperor bristled; Fan Feng and company glared with hatred yet hesitated to touch a scholar of his stature.

sentences.zh: 尋有河閒男子趙騰詣闕上書，指陳得失。

translations.literal: Soon there was a Hejian man Zhao Teng who went to the palace gate and submitted a memorial, pointing out gains and losses.
translations.idiomatic: Soon Zhao Teng of Hejian presented himself at the palace with a memorial criticizing policy.

sentences.zh: 帝發怒，遂收考詔獄，結以罔上不道。

translations.literal: The emperor blazed with anger; he seized Zhao and interrogated him in the imperial prison, sentencing him for deceiving superiors and lacking the Way.
translations.idiomatic: Enraged, the emperor jailed him under charges of deceiving the throne and grave disloyalty.

sentences.zh: 震復上疏救之曰：「臣聞堯舜之世，諫鼓謗木，立之於朝；

translations.literal: Zhen again submitted a memorial to rescue him, saying: 'Your subject has heard that in Yao and Shun's age, remonstrance drums and slander posts were set up in court;
translations.idiomatic: Yang pleaded for him: 'Under Yao and Shun the court set out drums for complaint and posts for criticism—'

sentences.zh: [一]殷周哲王，小人怨詈，則還自敬德。

translations.literal: [One] The wise kings of Yin and Zhou—when petty men reviled them, they returned to cultivating virtue.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Yet the sage kings of Yin and Zhou, when common folk railed at them, renewed their own virtue instead.'

sentences.zh: [二]所以達聰明，開不諱，博采負薪，盡極下情也。

translations.literal: [Two] By this they reached keen hearing and sight, opened no-taboo, broadly gathered firewood bearers, exhaustively reaching lower feelings.
translations.idiomatic: [2] They kept ears open, welcomed blunt speech, and even listened to woodcutters so nothing stayed hidden.'

sentences.zh: 今趙騰所坐激訐謗語為罪，與手刃犯法有差。

translations.literal: Now Zhao Teng's crime sits on harsh impeachment slanderous words—there is a difference from hand-blade breaking law.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao is punished for bitter words on paper—that is not the same as blood on a blade.'

sentences.zh: 乞為虧除，全騰之命，以誘芻蕘輿人之言。

translations.literal: I beg to reduce and remove, preserve Teng's life, to entice firewood grass cart men's words.
translations.idiomatic: Spare his life to encourage rustics and coachmen to speak freely.'

sentences.zh: 」[三]帝不省，騰竟伏屍都市。

translations.literal: ' [Three] The emperor did not examine; Teng in the end lay corpse in the great market.
translations.idiomatic: ' [3] The emperor ignored the plea; Zhao Teng was executed in the capital market.'

sentences.zh: 注[一]帝王紀曰：「堯置敢諫之鼓，舜立誹謗之木。」

translations.literal: [One] Di wang ji says: 'Yao set up a dare-remonstrance drum; Shun erected a slander post.'"
translations.idiomatic: [1] The Di wang ji records Yao's drum for dissenters and Shun's post for public criticism.'"

sentences.zh: 注[二]尚書曰「自殷王中宗及高宗及祖甲及我周文王，茲四人迪哲。

translations.literal: [Two] The Documents says: 'From Yin kings Zhongzong and Gaozong and Zujia and down to our Zhou King Wen—these four illuminated wisdom.
translations.idiomatic: [2] The Book of Documents lists Zhongzong, Gaozong, Zujia, and King Wen as four rulers who practiced discernment.'

sentences.zh: 厥或告之曰小人怨女詈女，則皇自敬德」也。

translations.literal: If someone tells them 'petty men resent you, curse you,' then grandly they themselves revere virtue.'"
translations.idiomatic: The text says that when told the people revile them, they turn inward and mend their virtue.'"

sentences.zh: 注[三]輿，觿也。

translations.literal: [Three] Yu (cart) is the same as xi (tally).
translations.idiomatic: [3] The gloss equates the cart-drivers (yu) with the tally-bearing youths (xi)—both stand for common folk.

sentences.zh: 詩曰：「詢於芻蕘。

translations.literal: The Odes say: 'Inquire of the firewood cutters.
translations.idiomatic: The Canon says: ask even the fuel-gatherers.'

sentences.zh: 」左氏傳曰「聽輿人之謀」也。

translations.literal: ' The Zuo Commentary says: 'Listen to cart men's counsel.'"
translations.idiomatic: ' The Zuo Commentary quotes listening to common coachmen.'"

sentences.zh: 會三年春，東巡岱宗，樊豐等因乘輿在外，競修第宅，震部掾高舒召大匠令史考校之，[一]得豐等所詐下詔書，具奏，須行還上之。

translations.literal: It happened in spring of the third year of Hui; there was an eastern tour of Mount Dai; Fan Feng and others, because the imperial carriage was abroad, vied to build mansions—Zhen's section clerk Gao Shu summoned the Director of Works scribe to examine them, [one] obtaining Feng and company's forged edicts; he fully memorialized, awaiting the progress's return to submit upward.
translations.idiomatic: In spring of the third year the emperor toured Mount Tai. Fan Feng used the absence of court to race ahead with palace mansions. Yang's aide Gao Shu had the Director of Works clerk audit the projects [1] and uncovered forged edicts; he prepared a full report to file when the ruler returned.

sentences.zh: 豐等聞，惶怖，會太史言星變逆行，遂共譖震云：「自趙騰死後，深用怨懟；

translations.literal: Feng and others heard, were panic-stricken; it happened the Grand Astrologer spoke of a star's change moving retrograde; they then together slandered Zhen, saying: 'Since Zhao Teng's death, he has deeply used resentment and rancor;
translations.idiomatic: Panicked, they waited until the court astrologer reported a retrograde omen, then all accused Yang: 'Since Zhao Teng's execution he has nursed a bitter grudge;'

sentences.zh: [二]且鄧氏故吏，有恚恨之心。

translations.literal: [Two] moreover as Deng clan's former subordinate, he has angry hatred's heart.
translations.idiomatic: [2] moreover he remains an old client of the Deng house and bears them malice.'

sentences.zh: 」[三]及車駕行還，便時太學，[四]夜遣使者策收震太尉印綬，於是柴門絕賓客。

translations.literal: ' [Three] When the carriage progress returned, choosing time at the Imperial Academy, [four] by night dispatched envoys with tally-recitation to seize Zhen's Grand Commandant seals and ribbons—thereupon brushwood gate cut off guests.
translations.idiomatic: ' [3] When the emperor reached Luoyang he paused at the Imperial Academy [4] and that night messengers stripped Yang of his seals. He barred his gate and refused all visitors.'

sentences.zh: 豐等復惡之，乃請大將軍耿寶奏震大臣不服罪，懷恚望，有詔遣歸本郡。

translations.literal: Feng and others again hated him; then requested Grand General Geng Bao memorialize Zhen as great minister not submitting guilt, harboring rancor and expectation—there was an edict dispatching him to return to his native commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Fan Feng pressed harder: through Grand General Geng Bao he accused Yang of defying sentence and nursing grievance. An edict sent him home to Hongnong.

sentences.zh: 震行至城西幾陽亭，乃慷慨謂其諸子門人曰：[五]「死者士之常分。

translations.literal: Zhen traveled reaching west of the city's Jiyang pavilion, then with stirring emotion said to his sons and disciples: [five] 'Death is the gentleman's constant lot.
translations.idiomatic: At the Jiyang post west of the city he told his sons and students: [5] 'A scholar's death is no accident;'

sentences.zh: 吾蒙恩居上司，疾奸臣狡猾而不能誅，惡嬖女傾亂而不能禁，何面目復見日月！

translations.literal: I have received favor dwelling in high office, hate crafty ministers' treachery yet cannot execute them, hate favorite women's overturning chaos yet cannot forbid—what face again to see sun and moon!
translations.idiomatic: I rose to the summit yet could not cut down treacherous ministers or halt palace women who shook the state—how dare I face heaven's lights again!'

sentences.zh: 身死之日，以雜木為棺，布單被裁足蓋形，勿歸頤次，勿設祭祠。

translations.literal: On the body's death day, use miscellaneous wood as coffin, cloth single cover cut sufficient to cover form—do not return burial mound, do not set sacrificial shrine.
translations.idiomatic: Bury me in a plain wooden coffin with a cloth sheet sized only to cover the body—no lavish tomb, no ancestral sacrifices.'

sentences.zh: 」因飲酖而卒，時年七十餘。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon he drank poisoned wine and died, aged over seventy.
translations.idiomatic: He took poison and died, past seventy years of age.

sentences.zh: 弘農太守移良[六]承樊豐等旨，遣吏於陝縣留停震喪，露棺道側，[七]□震諸子代郵行書，道路皆為隕涕。

translations.literal: Hongnong Governor Yi Liang [six] carrying Fan Feng and company's intent, dispatched clerks at Shan county to detain and halt Zhen's funeral, exposing coffin roadside; [seven] □ Zhen's sons replacing postal relay carrying letters—roadsides all for him shed tears.
translations.idiomatic: Governor Yi Liang of Hongnong [6] acted on Fan Feng's orders: he stopped the coffin in Shan county and left it open by the road [7]; his sons were driven [graph missing in source] to carry appeals like post riders—travelers wept along the way.

translations.literal: [Eight]
translations.idiomatic: [8] (endnote marker)

sentences.zh: 注[一]史謂府吏也。

translations.literal: [One] Scribe means office clerk.
translations.idiomatic: [1] 'Scribe' here means the yamen clerk.

sentences.zh: 注[二]懟，怨怒也。

translations.literal: [Two] Dui means resentful anger.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Dui denotes bitter resentment.

sentences.zh: 注[三]震初鄧騭辟之，故曰故吏。

translations.literal: [Three] Zhen was first recruited by Deng Zhi; hence called former subordinate.
translations.idiomatic: [3] Yang had first entered service under Deng Zhi—hence 'former client.'

sentences.zh: 注[四]且於太學待吉時而後入也，故曰便時。

translations.literal: [Four] Moreover at Imperial Academy awaiting auspicious hour then entering—hence called convenient season.
translations.idiomatic: [4] He halted at the academy until an auspicious moment—'choosing the hour' (bian shi).

sentences.zh: 前書「便時上林延壽門」也。

translations.literal: Former Book 'convenient season Shanglin Yanshou gate.'"
translations.idiomatic: As in the Han shu phrase about entering through Shanglin's Yanshou gate at the chosen hour.'"

sentences.zh: 注[五]慷慨，悲歎。

translations.literal: [Five] Kangkai means mournful sigh.
translations.idiomatic: [5] Kangkai: grieving aloud.

sentences.zh: 注[六]風俗通曰：「齊公子雍食菜於移，其後氏焉。」

translations.literal: [Six] Fengsu tong says: 'Qi prince Yong ate fief at Yi—later generations took it as surname.'"
translations.idiomatic: [6] The Fengsu tong traces the surname Yi to Prince Yong of Qi.'"

sentences.zh: 注[七]謝承書曰：「震臨沒，謂諸子以牛車薄簀，載柩還歸。

translations.literal: [Seven] Xie Cheng's book says: 'Zhen facing death, told sons with ox cart thin mat to carry bier returning.
translations.idiomatic: [7] Xie Cheng records Yang instructing his sons to haul the coffin home on an ox cart with only a thin pallet—'

sentences.zh: 」 注[八]說文：「郵，境上行書捨也。

translations.literal: ' [Eight] Shuowen: 'You—relay station on borders for carrying letters lodging.
translations.idiomatic: ' [8] The Shuowen defines you as a postal lodge on the frontier road.'

sentences.zh: 」廣雅曰：「郵，驛也。」

translations.literal: ' Guangya says: 'You—relay.'"
translations.idiomatic: ' The Guangya glosses you as a courier station.'"

sentences.zh: 注[一]墓在今潼關西大道之北，其碑尚存。

translations.literal: [One] Tomb is north of the great road west of present Tong Pass—its stele still survives.
translations.idiomatic: [1] The tomb lies north of the highway west of Tong Pass; the stele still stands.

sentences.zh: 注[二]續漢書曰：「大鳥來止亭樹，下地安行到柩前，正立低頭淚出。

translations.literal: [Two] Continuation Han Book says: 'A great bird came alighting on pavilion tree, descended earth calmly walking to bier front, stood upright lowering head tears issuing.
translations.idiomatic: [2] The Xu Han shu tells of a great bird that perched on the station tree, walked calmly to the coffin, stood before it, bowed its head, and wept—'

sentences.zh: 觿人更共摩撫抱持，終不驚駭。

translations.literal: Ivory tally youths together rubbed stroked embraced held—throughout not startled.
translations.idiomatic: Townsmen stroked and steadied it; it showed no fear.'

sentences.zh: 」謝承書曰：「其鳥五色，高丈餘，兩翼長二丈三尺，人莫知其名也。」

translations.literal: ' Xie Cheng's book says: 'That bird five-colored, height over one zhang, two wings length two zhang three chi—people none knew its name.'"
translations.idiomatic: ' Xie Cheng adds that it stood over a zhang tall, multicolored, with wings two zhang three chi—no one knew its species.'"

sentences.zh: 注[三]藩，樊也。

translations.literal: [Three] Fan is fence.
translations.idiomatic: [3] Fan means fence or enclosure.

sentences.zh: 詩云：「營營青蠅，止於樊，愷悌君子，無信讒言。

translations.literal: The Odes say: 'Buzzing green flies stop at the fence—kind gentle lord, do not trust slander words.
translations.idiomatic: The ode warns: blue flies swarm the fence—true gentlemen never heed slander.'

sentences.zh: 」青蠅，污白使黑，污黑使白，喻佞人變亂善惡也。

translations.literal: ' Green flies—defile white making black, defile black making white—likens flattering men overturning good and evil.
translations.idiomatic: ' Flies smear white to black and black to white—like flatterers who invert right and wrong.'

sentences.zh: 注[四]禮記曰：「孔子將終，歌曰：『泰山其頹乎！

translations.literal: [Four] Book of Rites says: 'Confucius about to end, sang: 'Mount Tai—about to crumble!
translations.idiomatic: [4] The Book of Rites records Confucius' final song: 'Mount Tai may crumble!'

sentences.zh: 梁木其壞乎！

translations.literal: The ridge beam—about to break!
translations.idiomatic: The great beam may snap!'

sentences.zh: 』」震之被譖也，高舒亦得罪，以減死論。

translations.literal: '" When Zhen was slandered, Gao Shu also received guilt, sentenced with reduced death.
translations.idiomatic: ' When Yang fell under accusation, Gao Shu was sentenced to commute death to penal labor.'

sentences.zh: 及震事顯，舒拜侍御史，至荊州刺史。

translations.literal: When Zhen's affair became manifest, Shu was appointed Attending Secretary, reaching Jing province Inspector.
translations.idiomatic: After Yang was vindicated, Gao Shu rose to attending secretary and eventually inspector of Jing province.

sentences.zh: 震五子。

translations.literal: Zhen had five sons.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Zhen had five sons.

sentences.zh: 長子牧，富波相。

translations.literal: Eldest son Mu, Fubo Chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: The eldest, Mu, served as chancellor of Fubo county.

translations.literal: [One]
translations.idiomatic: [1] (note marker)

sentences.zh: 注[一]富波，縣，屬汝南郡。

translations.literal: [One] Fubo—a county belonging to Runan commandery.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Fubo was a county in Runan commandery.

sentences.zh: 牧孫奇，靈帝時為侍中，帝嘗從容問奇曰：「朕何如桓帝？

translations.literal: Mu's grandson Qi, in Emperor Ling's time was Palace Attendant; emperor once leisurely asked Qi: 'How do We compare to Emperor Huan?
translations.idiomatic: Mu's grandson Qi became a palace attendant under Emperor Ling. One day the emperor casually asked how he compared with Emperor Huan.

sentences.zh: 」對曰：「陛下之於桓帝，亦猶虞舜比德唐堯。

translations.literal: ' He replied: 'Your Majesty compared to Emperor Huan is also like Yu Shun measuring virtue against Tang Yao.
translations.idiomatic: ' Qi answered, 'Your Majesty stands to Emperor Huan as Shun beside Yao.'

sentences.zh: 」帝不悅曰：「卿強項，真楊震子孫，[一]死後必復致大鳥矣。

translations.literal: ' Emperor displeased said: 'You stiff-neck—truly Yang Zhen's descendant; [one] after death surely again will bring great bird.
translations.idiomatic: ' The emperor snapped, 'Stubborn as they say—every inch Yang Zhen's heir [1]—you'll summon another giant bird when you die!'

sentences.zh: 」出為汝南太守。

translations.literal: ' Sent out as Runan Governor.
translations.idiomatic: ' He was banished to governor of Runan.

sentences.zh: 帝崩後，復入為侍中□尉，從獻帝西遷，有功勤。

translations.literal: After emperor's death, again entered as Palace Attendant □ Wei; followed Emperor Xian westward migration, had merit diligence.
translations.idiomatic: After Ling died he returned as palace attendant and [missing office graph] captain and followed Emperor Xian west, earning merit.

sentences.zh: 及李傕脅帝歸其營，奇與黃門侍郎鐘繇誘傕部曲將宋曄﹑楊昂令反傕，傕由此孤弱，帝乃得東。

translations.literal: When Li Jue coerced emperor returning to his camp, Qi with Yellow Gate Gentleman Zhong Yao enticed Jue's division commanders Song Ye, Yang Ang ordering them turn against Jue—Jue from this isolated weakened; emperor then could east.
translations.idiomatic: When Li Jue dragged the emperor to his camp, Qi and Zhong Yao turned Jue's officers Song Ye and Yang Ang against him, isolating Li so the sovereign could flee east.

sentences.zh: [二]後徙都許，追封奇子亮為陽成亭侯。

translations.literal: [Two] Later moved capital to Xu, posthumously enfeoffed Qi's son Liang as Yangcheng village Marquis.
translations.idiomatic: [2] After the court moved to Xu, Qi's son Liang was posthumously enfeoffed as village marquis of Yangcheng.

translations.literal: [Three]
translations.idiomatic: [3] (note marker)

sentences.zh: 注[一]強項，言不低屈也，光武謂董宣為「強項令」也。

translations.literal: [One] Qiangxiang means not bowing low—Guangwu called Dong Xuan 'stiff-neck magistrate.'"
translations.idiomatic: [1] 'Stiff-necked' means refusing to bend—the label Guangwu gave Magistrate Dong Xuan.'"

sentences.zh: 注[二]魏志曰，繇為黃門侍郎，傕脅天子，繇與尚書郎韓斌同策謀。

translations.literal: [Two] Wei zhi says: Yao was Yellow Gate Gentleman; Jue coerced Son of Heaven; Yao with Masters of Writing Gentleman Han Bin together plotted strategy.
translations.idiomatic: [2] The Wei zhi states that Zhong Yao, as Yellow Gate Gentleman, plotted with Han Bin of the Masters of Writing when Li Jue held the emperor hostage.

sentences.zh: 天子得出長安，繇有力焉。

translations.literal: Son of Heaven could exit Chang'an—Yao had strength therein.
translations.idiomatic: Zhong Yao's hand helped the emperor escape Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 注[三]亮舊宅在閿鄉縣西南。

translations.literal: [Three] Liang's old residence is southwest of Wenxiang county.
translations.idiomatic: [3] Liang's former estate lay southwest of Wenxiang county.

sentences.zh: 震少子奉，奉子敷，篤志博聞，議者以為能世其家。

translations.literal: Zhen's youngest son Feng; Feng's son Fu—steadfast aim and broad hearing—discussers deemed able to continue his household.
translations.idiomatic: The youngest son Feng and grandson Fu were devoted scholars; contemporaries thought them worthy heirs to the Yang tradition.

sentences.zh: 敷早卒，子觿，亦傳先業，以謁者僕射從獻帝入關，累遷御史中丞。

translations.literal: Fu died early; son Xi also transmitted former enterprises—with Supervisor of Petitioners followed Emperor Xuan into pass, accumulated promotions to Palace Assistant Secretary.
translations.idiomatic: Fu died young; his son Xi upheld the family legacy. As supervisor of petitioners he followed Emperor Xian west through the passes and rose to palace assistant secretary.

sentences.zh: 及帝東還，夜走度河，觿率諸官屬步從至太陽，拜侍中。

translations.literal: When emperor east returned, by night fled crossing river—Xi led various officials' subordinates walking following to Taiyang, appointed Palace Attendant.
translations.idiomatic: When the court fled east by night across the Yellow River, Xi walked his staff all the way to Taiyang and was named palace attendant.

sentences.zh: [一]建安二年，追前功封蓩亭侯。

translations.literal: [One] Jian'an second year, tracing former merit enfeoffed Mao village Marquis.
translations.idiomatic: [1] In Jian'an 2 he received retroactive credit for his service with a village marquisate at Mao.

translations.literal: [Two]
translations.idiomatic: [2] (note marker)

sentences.zh: 注[一]太陽，縣，屬河東郡。

translations.literal: [One] Taiyang—a county belonging to Hedong commandery.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Taiyang lay in Hedong commandery.

sentences.zh: 注[二]郡國志桃林縣有蓩鄉，音莫老反。

translations.literal: [Two] Commandery State Treatise: Taolin county has Mao village, sound mo-lao fan.
translations.idiomatic: [2] The treatise locates Mao village in Taolin county, read mo-lao.

sentences.zh: 震中子秉。

translations.literal: Zhen's middle son Bing.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Zhen's second son was Yang Bing.

sentences.zh: 秉字叔節，少傳父業，兼明京氏易，博通書傳，常隱居教授。

translations.literal: Bing, courtesy Shujie, in youth transmitted father's enterprise, also clarified Jing clan Changes, broadly penetrated documents and commentaries, often lived in seclusion teaching.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Bing, styled Shujie, inherited his father's learning, mastered the Jing-school Book of Changes, read widely, and for years taught in reclusion.

sentences.zh: 年四十餘，乃應司空辟，拜待御史，頻出為豫﹑荊﹑徐﹑兗四州刺史，遷任城相。

translations.literal: Age over forty, then answered Minister of Works recruitment, appointed Awaiting Secretary, repeatedly went out as Yu, Jing, Xu, Yan four provinces' Inspectors, transferred to Rencheng Chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: After forty he took the Minister of Works' summons, became an awaiting secretary, then served in turn as inspector of Yu, Jing, Xu, and Yan and as chancellor of Rencheng.

sentences.zh: 自為刺史﹑二千石，計日受奉，余祿不入私門。

translations.literal: From being Inspector, two-thousand-shi, he counted days receiving salary—surplus emoluments did not enter private gate.
translations.idiomatic: As inspector and two-thousand-shi official he drew pay strictly by the calendar and never pocketed extra into his household.

sentences.zh: 故吏繼錢百萬遺之，閉門不受。

translations.literal: Former subordinates in succession money a million to present him—closed gate not accepting.
translations.idiomatic: When old subordinates tried to give him a million in cash, he barred his gate.

sentences.zh: 以廉潔稱。

translations.literal: By incorruption praised.
translations.idiomatic: He was celebrated for integrity.

sentences.zh: 桓帝即位，以明尚書征入勸講，[一]拜太中大夫﹑左中郎將，遷侍中﹑尚書。

translations.literal: When Emperor Huan assumed throne, by clarify Documents summoned in encouraging lecture, [one] appointed Grand Household Grandee, Left General of the Gentlemen of the Household, transferred Palace Attendant, Masters of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Huan mounted the throne he summoned Yang for his expertise in the Documents [1] as lecturer-in-residence, named him grand household grandee and left general of the gentlemen of the household, then palace attendant and master of writing.

sentences.zh: 帝時微行，私過幸河南尹梁胤府捨。

translations.literal: Emperor at times traveled incognito, privately visited passing favored Henan Governor Liang Yin's mansion lodging.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor sometimes slipped out incognito to stay privately at the mansion of Liang Yin, governor of Henan.

sentences.zh: [二]是日大風拔樹，晝昏，秉因上疏諫曰：

translations.literal: [Two] That day great wind uprooted trees, daytime dark—Bing therefore submitted memorial remonstrating saying:
translations.idiomatic: [2] That day a gale tore up trees and noon turned dark; Yang Bing presented a memorial:

sentences.zh: 臣聞瑞由德至，□應事生。

translations.literal: Your subject has heard auspicious omens arrive through virtue—□ responds affairs arising.
translations.idiomatic: I am taught that auspicious omens follow virtue and prodigies answer human deeds [graph missing in source].

sentences.zh: 傳曰：『禍福無門，唯人所召。

translations.literal: Tradition says: 'Calamity and blessing have no gate—only what men summon.
translations.idiomatic: The tradition runs: weal and woe have no door—we summon them ourselves.'

sentences.zh: 』[三]天不言語，以□異譴告，是以孔子迅雷風烈必有變動。

translations.literal: ' [Three] Heaven does not speak—with □ prodigy reproof warns; hence Confucius sudden thunder fierce wind surely has change movement.
translations.idiomatic: ' [3] Heaven speaks through omens [graph missing]; therefore Confucius said thunder or fierce wind portends change.'

sentences.zh: 詩云：『敬天之威，不敢驅馳。

translations.literal: The Odes say: 'Revere Heaven's awe—dare not gallop rush.
translations.idiomatic: The Odes say: stand in awe of Heaven's majesty and never rush about lightly.'

sentences.zh: 』[四] 王者至尊，出入有常，警蹕而行，靜室而止，[五]自非郊廟之事，則鑾旗不駕。

translations.literal: ' [Four] The king is most honored—going out and entering have constancy, alarm-clearance and proceed, quiet chamber and stop; [five] if not suburban-altar temple affairs, then bell-banner carriages not driven.
translations.idiomatic: ' [4] A sovereign travels by fixed ritual—roads cleared, quarters swept [5]—and rolls out chariots only for suburban sacrifices or ancestral shrines.'

sentences.zh: [六]故詩稱『自郊徂宮』，[七]易曰『王假有廟，致孝享也』。

translations.literal: [Six] Hence Odes praises 'from suburb to palace'; [seven] Changes says 'king arrives at having temple—bringing utmost filial offering.'"
translations.idiomatic: [6] Hence the ode moves 'from the meadow rite to the ancestral hall'; [7] the Changes says the king 'draws near the shrine' to offer devotion.'"

sentences.zh: [八]諸侯如臣之家，春秋尚列其誡，[九]況以先王法服而私出盤遊！

translations.literal: [Eight] Marquises visiting ministers' houses—the Spring and Autumn Annals still tabulates their warnings; [nine] how much more with Former Kings' ritual robes while privately going roaming!
translations.idiomatic: [8] The Annals fault even feudal lords who slip into ministers' houses; [9] how grave when the Son of Heaven trades ritual robes for secret pleasure trips!'

sentences.zh: [一０]降亂尊卑，等威無序，[一一]侍□守空宮，紱璽委女妾，設有非常之變，任章之謀，[一二]上負先帝，下悔靡及。
