translations.literal: [Ten] Lowering chaos to noble-base, ranks awe without order; [eleven] attendants □ guard empty palace, seals ribbons entrusted women concubines—if happen extraordinary change, Ren Zhang's plot—[twelve] above betray Former Emperor, below regret not reaching.
translations.idiomatic: [10] High and low blur and ceremonial rank collapses; [11] attendants [graph missing] guard empty halls while seals sit with concubines—should crisis strike like Ren Zhang's plot, [12] you wrong your ancestors above and regret below will come too late.'

sentences.zh: 臣奕世受恩，[一三]得備納言，[一四] 又以薄學，充在講勤，特蒙哀識，見照日月，恩重命輕，義使士死，敢憚摧折，略陳其愚。

translations.literal: Your subject successive generations receive favor, [thirteen] obtained readiness speech intake; [fourteen] moreover by shallow learning, fill in lecture diligence, specially receives pity recognition, seen illuminated sun moon—favor heavy life light, righteousness makes gentlemen die—dare shrink from crushing breaking—briefly display his stupidity.
translations.idiomatic: My house owes generations of grace [13]; I hold office among those who speak truth to power [14]. Though my learning is slight I lecture at court and enjoy sunlight from your favor—obligation outweighs life—so though this may destroy me I venture these blunt words.'

sentences.zh: 帝不納。

translations.literal: Emperor did not accept.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor brushed him aside.

sentences.zh: 秉以病乞退，出為右扶風。

translations.literal: Bing by illness begged retirement, went out as Right Fufeng.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Bing pleaded illness and took assignment as governor of Right Fufeng.

sentences.zh: 太尉黃瓊惜其去朝廷，上秉勸講帷幄，不宜外遷，留拜光祿大夫。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Huang Qiong regretted his leaving court, memorialized Bing encouraging lecture tent curtain—not fitting outer transfer—retained appointed Household Grandee.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Commandant Huang Qiong argued he should stay as imperial lecturer rather than leave the capital and kept him as household grandee.

sentences.zh: 是時大將軍梁冀用權，秉稱病。

translations.literal: At this time Grand General Liang Ji wielded power—Bing claimed illness.
translations.idiomatic: While Grand General Liang Ji dominated court, Yang Bing feigned illness.

sentences.zh: 六年，冀誅後，乃拜太僕，遷太常。

translations.literal: Sixth year, after Ji executed, then appointed Grand Coachman, transferred Chamberlain for Ceremonials.
translations.idiomatic: Six years later, after Liang Ji fell, he accepted posts as grand coachman and then chamberlain for ceremonials.

sentences.zh: 注[一]勸講，猶侍講也。

translations.literal: [One] Encouraging lecture is like attendant lecture.
translations.idiomatic: [1] 'Encouraging lecture' equals lecturer-in-waiting.

sentences.zh: 注[二]胤，梁冀子也。

translations.literal: [Two] Yin is Liang Ji's son.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Liang Yin was Liang Ji's son.

sentences.zh: 注[三]左傳閔子馬之詞。

translations.literal: [Three] Zuo Commentary Min Zima's words.
translations.idiomatic: [3] A line from Min Zima in the Zuo Commentary.

sentences.zh: 注[四]詩大雅曰「敬天之怒，無敢戲豫，敬天之渝，無敢馳驅」，與此文稍異也。

translations.literal: [Four] Greater Ya says 'Revere Heaven's anger—dare not playful ease; revere Heaven's change—dare not gallop drive'—with this text slightly differs.
translations.idiomatic: [4] The canonical ode reads 'Revere Heaven's wrath—no idle sport; heed Heaven's shifts—no reckless riding,' slightly different from Yang's quotation.

sentences.zh: 注[五]蹕，止行人也。

translations.literal: [Five] Bi stops travelers.
translations.idiomatic: [5] Bi clears the thoroughfare of pedestrians.

sentences.zh: 靜室謂先使清宮也。

translations.literal: Quiet chamber means first ordering palace cleared.
translations.idiomatic: 'Quiet chamber' means the palace was swept before the ruler rested.

sentences.zh: 前書音義曰，漢有靜室令也。

translations.literal: Former Book sounds meanings says: Han had Quiet Chamber Director.
translations.idiomatic: The Han shu glossary notes a Director of the Quiet Chamber.

sentences.zh: 注[六]漢官儀曰「前驅有雲罕，皮軒鑾旗車」也。

translations.literal: [Six] Han Guan Yi says 'forward escort has cloud-net, leather-canopy bell-banner carriage.'"
translations.idiomatic: [6] The Han Guan Yi describes heralds with cloud banners and bell-decked chariots.'"

sentences.zh: 注[七]詩大雅雲漢之詞也。

translations.literal: [Seven] Greater Ya 'Granary of Clouds' words.
translations.idiomatic: [7] From the Greater Ya ode 'Granary of Clouds.'

sentences.zh: 郊，祭天也。

translations.literal: Jiao is sacrifice Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: The suburban rite sacrifices to Heaven.

sentences.zh: 注[八]萃卦詞也。

translations.literal: [Eight] Gathering hexagram words.
translations.idiomatic: [8] A line from the Gathering hexagram.

sentences.zh: 假，至也。

translations.literal: Jia means arrive.
translations.idiomatic: Jia means 'to arrive.'

sentences.zh: 假音格。

translations.literal: Jia sound ge.
translations.idiomatic: Read jia like ge.

sentences.zh: 注[九]左傳，齊莊公如崔杼之家，為杼所殺也，注[一０]法服謂天子服，日、月、星辰、山、龍、華蟲、藻、火、粉、米、*[黼、黻]*十二章。

translations.literal: [Nine] Zuo Commentary—Qi Duke Zhuang went to Cui Zhu's house, was killed by Zhu; [ten] ritual robes mean Son of Heaven robes—sun, moon, stars, mountains, dragons, splendid insects, algae, fire, powder, rice, *[fu, fu]* twelve chapters.
translations.idiomatic: [9] Duke Zhuang of Qi died visiting Cui Zhu; [10] 'ritual robes' are the twelve-pattern imperial gown—sun, moon, constellations, mountains, dragon, pheasant, algae, fire, rice, grain, *[axes and fu motifs]*.

sentences.zh: 注[一一]等威謂威儀有等差也。

translations.literal: [Eleven] Deng wei means awe-bearing has gradations.
translations.idiomatic: [11] 'Ranked awe' means prescribed ceremonial rank.

sentences.zh: 左傳曰「貴有常尊，賤有等威」也。

translations.literal: Zuo Commentary says 'noble has constant honor, base has deng wei.'"
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Commentary says the noble retain dignity and the humble keep their station.'"

sentences.zh: 注[一二]前書曰，代郡太守任宣坐謀反誅，宣子章為公車丞，亡在渭城界中，夜玄服入廟，居郎閒，執戟立於廟門，待上至，欲為逆，發覺伏誅也。

translations.literal: [Twelve] Former Book says: Dai commandery Governor Ren Xuan sat plotting rebellion executed; Xuan's son Zhang was Imperial Carriage Assistant, fled in Weicheng borderland, night dark robes entered temple, dwelt among gentleman-attendants, grasped halberd stood at temple gate, awaited superior's arrival, wished to do rebellion—discovered executed.
translations.idiomatic: [12] The Han shu tells of Ren Zhang, son of the rebel governor Ren Xuan, who hid in Weicheng, slipped into the shrine in black, posed among the attendants with a halberd, and waited to assassinate the emperor—he was caught and killed.'

sentences.zh: 注[一三]奕猶重也。

translations.literal: [Thirteen] Yi means heavy/layered.
translations.idiomatic: [13] Yi here means 'accumulated' or 'successive.'

sentences.zh: 注[一四]納言，尚書。

translations.literal: [Fourteen] Speech intake—Masters of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: [14] 'Speech intake' denotes the Masters of Writing.

sentences.zh: 延熹三年，白馬令李雲以諫受罪，秉爭之不能得，坐免官，歸田裡。

translations.literal: In the third year of Yanxi, Baima county Magistrate Li Yun suffered punishment for remonstrating—Bing disputed it but could not prevail; sat dismissed office, returned to fields.
translations.idiomatic: In Yanxi 3 Li Yun of Baima was punished for criticizing the throne. Yang Bing defended him in vain, lost his post, and went home.

sentences.zh: [一]其年冬，復徵拜河南尹。

translations.literal: [One] That year winter, again summoned appointed Henan Governor.
translations.idiomatic: [1] The same winter he was recalled as governor of Henan.

sentences.zh: 先是中常侍單超弟匡為濟陰太守，以臧罪為刺史第五種所劾，窘急，乃賂客任方刺兗州從事□羽。

translations.literal: Earlier Palace Attendant Shan Chao's younger brother Kuang was Jiyin Governor—by bribery crime impeached by Inspector Di Wuzhong; pressed tight, then bribed guest Ren Fang to assassinate Yan province Adjutant □ Yu.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier Shan Kuang, brother of the eunuch Shan Chao, had been impeached for corruption by Inspector Di Wuzhong. Desperate, he paid Ren Fang to murder the Yanzhou adjutant [name damaged].

sentences.zh: 事已見種傳。

translations.literal: The affair already appears in Zhong's biography.
translations.idiomatic: The full story is told in the biography of Di Wuzhong.

sentences.zh: 及捕得方，囚系洛陽，匡慮秉當窮竟其事，密令方等得突獄亡走。

translations.literal: When Fang was captured, imprisoned in Luoyang, Kuang feared Bing would exhaust investigate the affair—secretly ordered Fang and others could burst prison flee.
translations.idiomatic: When Ren Fang was jailed in Luoyang, Shan Kuang feared Yang Bing would expose the plot and secretly arranged a breakout.

sentences.zh: 尚書召秉詰責，秉對曰：「春秋不誅黎比而魯多盜，[二]方等無狀，釁由單匡。

translations.literal: Masters of Writing summoned Bing to question blame—Bing replied: 'Spring and Autumn did not execute Li Bi yet Lu had many robbers; [two] Fang and company lack shape—the flaw springs from Shan Kuang.
translations.idiomatic: The Masters of Writing challenged Yang Bing. He answered: 'The Annals did not execute Li Bi yet Lu filled with bandits [2]; Ren Fang's crimes stem from Shan Kuang.'

sentences.zh: 刺執法之吏，害奉公之臣，復令逃竄，寬縱罪身，元惡大憝，終為國害。

translations.literal: Assassinate law-enforcing clerks, harm public-serving ministers—again letting flee hide, broadly indulging guilty bodies—chief evil great villain, in the end becomes state's harm.
translations.idiomatic: They attacked officers of the law and loyal ministers, then let the killers escape—shielding guilt and nurturing the arch-villain who will ruin the state.'

sentences.zh: 乞檻車征匡考核其事，則奸慝蹤緒，必可立得。

translations.literal: Beg caged cart summon Kuang examine verify the affair—then crafty wicked traces and threads can surely be immediately obtained.
translations.idiomatic: Send a prison cart for Shan Kuang and examine him—the whole conspiracy will surface at once.'

sentences.zh: 」而秉竟坐輸作左校，以久旱赦出。

translations.literal: ' Yet Bing in the end sat sentenced labor Left School—by long drought amnesty released.

translations.idiomatic: [1] Xie Cheng records that after dismissal Yang lived in austere poverty, eating only every other day—'

sentences.zh: 任城故孝廉景慮繼錢百餘萬，就以餉秉，秉閉門距絕不受。」

translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse prompted Huangfu Gui and others to urge reinstatement of the upright Yang Bing.

translations.literal: There was edict Imperial Carriage summon Bing and recluse Wei Zhu—two men each claimed illness not arriving.
translations.idiomatic: An edict summoned Yang Bing and the recluse Wei Zhu by imperial carriage; both pleaded illness.

translations.literal: Authorities together impeached Bing, Zhu great irreverence—requested send down subordinates rectify their crimes.
translations.idiomatic: Officials charged both with grave disrespect and asked local authorities to sentence them.

sentences.zh: 尚書令周景與尚書邊韶議奏：「秉儒學侍講，常在謙虛；

translations.literal: Masters of Writing Director Zhou Jing with Master of Writing Bian Shao deliberated memorial: 'Bing Ru learning attendant lecture—always in modesty;
translations.idiomatic: Director Zhou Jing and Bian Shao replied: 'Yang Bing is a scholar-lecturer of retiring habits;'

sentences.zh: 著隱居行義，以退讓為節。

translations.literal: Zhu lives reclusion practicing righteousness—takes yielding retreat as restraint.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Zhu lives by integrity in seclusion and prizes yielding.'

sentences.zh: 俱徵不至，誠違側席之望，然逶迤退食，足抑苟進之風。

translations.literal: Both summoned not arriving—indeed violates side-mat expectation—yet winding retreat eating—sufficient to suppress rash advance wind.
translations.idiomatic: Their refusal disappoints the court's eager search for talent, yet their leisurely withdrawal curbs opportunists.'

sentences.zh: [一]夫明王之世，必有不召之臣，[二]聖朝弘養，宜用優游之禮。

translations.literal: [One] In enlightened king's age—surely are not-summoned ministers; [two] sage court magnanimously nurtures—should apply leisurely roaming ritual.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Even enlightened ages keep ministers who refuse summons; [2] our dynasty should honor them with patience.'

sentences.zh: 可告在所屬，喻以朝庭恩意。

translations.literal: May inform located subordinates—instruct with court dynasty grace intent.
translations.idiomatic: Let local officials explain the court's goodwill.'

sentences.zh: 如遂不至，詳議其罰。

translations.literal: If finally not arriving—detailed deliberate their punishment.
translations.idiomatic: If they still refuse, then debate punishment.'

sentences.zh: 」於是重征，乃到，拜太常。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon heavy summon—they arrived—appointed Chamberlain for Ceremonials.
translations.idiomatic: ' A second summons brought them in; Yang Bing became chamberlain for ceremonials.'

sentences.zh: 注[一]詩國風羔羊詩曰：「退食自公，委憨委憨。

translations.literal: [One] Airs of the States Lamb Hide poem says: 'Withdraw eating from duke—wei han wei han.
translations.idiomatic: [1] The 'Lamb Hide' ode reads 'withdraw from court meals, serene and easy' (the received text repeats a rare binome where standard editions have 'serpentine ease').

sentences.zh: 」退食謂減膳也。

translations.literal: ' Withdraw eating means reduce meals.
translations.idiomatic: ' 'Withdraw for meals' means taking simpler fare after court.'

sentences.zh: 從於公謂正直順於事也。

translations.literal: Following from duke means upright smooth in affairs.
translations.idiomatic: 'From the duke' means conducting oneself uprightly in office.'

sentences.zh: 委憨，委曲自得之貌。

translations.literal: Wei han—bending self-content appearance.
translations.idiomatic: The repeated phrase describes easy, unforced poise (variant graphs in received text).

sentences.zh: 注[二]堯時許由，禹時伯成子高，湯時務光等。

translations.literal: [Two] Yao times Xu You; Yu times Bocheng Zigao; Tang times Wuguang and others.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Examples include Xu You under Yao, Bocheng Zigao under Yu, and Wuguang under Tang.'

sentences.zh: 五年冬，代劉矩為太尉。

translations.literal: Fifth year winter, replaced Liu Ju as Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In winter of the fifth Yanxi year he succeeded Liu Ju as grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 是時宦官方熾，任人及子弟為官，[一]佈滿天下，競為貪淫，朝野嗟怨。

translations.literal: At this time eunuch faction just blazing—appoint men and sons brothers as officials; [one] spread fill under Heaven—vie to be greedy licentious—court and wild sigh resentment.
translations.idiomatic: Eunuchs placed kin and clients everywhere [1]; greed ran riot and court and countryside groaned.

sentences.zh: 秉與司空周景上言：「內外吏職，多非其人，自頃所征，皆特拜不試，致盜竊縱恣，怨訟紛錯。

translations.literal: Bing with Minister of Works Zhou Jing upward spoke: 'Inner and outer clerk posts—many not their persons—since recently those summoned—all specially appointed not tested—leading theft robbery reckless license—resentment lawsuits tangled wrong.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Bing and Minister of Works Zhou Jing reported: 'Inside and out the wrong men hold office—lately appointments skip probation, embolden criminals, and clog the courts with suits.'

sentences.zh: 舊典，中臣子弟不得居位秉埶，而今枝葉賓客布列職署，或年少庸人，典據守宰，上下忿患，四方愁毒。

translations.literal: Old canons—inner ministers' sons brothers may not dwell posts grasp power—yet now branch-leaf guests spread arrange duty offices—or young petty men—classic occupy shepherd magistrate—high low resent illness—four quarters sorrow poison.
translations.idiomatic: Statute barred inner-court families from power, yet clients and kin pack every yamen—young mediocrities rule provinces and counties while resentment spreads everywhere.'

sentences.zh: 可遵用舊章，退貪殘，塞□謗。

translations.literal: May follow employ old statutes—dismiss greedy cruel—block □ slander.
translations.idiomatic: Reapply the old rules: purge the corrupt and choke off slander [graph missing].'

sentences.zh: 請下司隸校尉、中二千石、二千石、城門五營校尉、北軍中候，各實核所部，應當斥罷，自以狀言，三府廉察有遺漏，續上。

translations.literal: Request send down Metropolitan Governor, full two-thousand-shi, two-thousand-shi, City Gates Five Camps colonels, Northern Army middle marquis—each truthfully verify subordinates—should dismiss remove—self by document speak—Three Offices honest inspect have omissions—continue upward.
translations.idiomatic: Order the metropolitan commandant, ranking ministers, commandery governors, city-gate commanders, and northern army inspectors to audit their jurisdictions, dismiss the unfit, report in writing, and send follow-ups if the Three Offices miss anyone.'

sentences.zh: 」帝從之。

translations.literal: ' Emperor followed it.
translations.idiomatic: ' The emperor agreed.'

sentences.zh: 於是秉條奏牧守以下匈奴中郎將燕瑗、青州刺史羊亮、遼東太守孫諠等五十餘人，或死或免，天下莫不肅然。

translations.idiomatic: Yang Bing impeached more than fifty officials from colonels Yan Yuan and Yang Liang down to county magistrates—some executed, some cashiered—and the realm stood in awe.

sentences.zh: 注[一]任謂保任。

translations.literal: [One] Ren means guarantee appointment.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Ren refers to sponsored nomination.

translations.idiomatic: County clerks were routinely kept as gentlemen-at-court; Yang reported over seven hundred idle gentlemen [1], empty treasuries, swarms of sinecures, and corrupt governors turning provinces into pools to rinse away scandal.'

translations.literal: Should cut off sideways appointment—to block coveting expectation's tip.

translations.literal: [Two] From this end Emperor Huan age—accounting clerks none again retained appointed.

translations.literal: [One] Three Departments Gentlemen—explanation see Emperor An annals.

translations.literal: ' Du Yu commentary says: 'None hope gaze upper position.'"
translations.idiomatic: ' Du Yu glosses: no ambition for another's throne.'"

sentences.zh: 七年，南巡園陵，特詔秉從。

translations.idiomatic: Zhang Biao of Nanyang, an old patron from the emperor's youth, levied extra taxes ahead of the tour and pocketed most of it.

translations.idiomatic: [1] At Nanyang the entourage traded favors freely and edicts showered appointments.'

translations.literal: Xi Tao warns Yu—lies in regulating men.

translations.literal: Should cut unwilling grace—to sever seeking desire's road.

translations.idiomatic: [1] Nanyang was part of Jing province.

sentences.zh: 明王奉順此道，建國設都。」

translations.literal: Enlightened king obeys this way—establishes state sets capital.'"
translations.idiomatic: The wise king follows that pattern in building the realm.'"

sentences.zh: 積，聚也。

sentences.zh: 」詩小雅曰：「皎皎白駒，食我□苗，所謂伊人，於焉逍遙。

translations.literal: ' It speaks King Xuan's offices lost their men—worthies rode white colt and departed them.
translations.idiomatic: ' It means King Xuan's court lost good men, who departed like the white colt of the ode.'"

translations.idiomatic: Hou Lan's brother Can was inspector of Yi province, notorious for embezzlement and terrorizing the commandery.

sentences.zh: 明年，秉劾奏參，檻車征詣廷尉。

translations.literal: Next year, Bing impeached memorialized Can—caged cart summoned to Commandant of Justice.
translations.idiomatic: The next year Yang Bing impeached him and had him hauled to the capital in a prison wagon for the Commandant of Justice.

sentences.zh: 參惶恐，道自殺。

translations.literal: Can panic-struck—on road killed self.
translations.idiomatic: Can killed himself en route.

sentences.zh: [一]秉因奏覽及中常侍具瑗曰：

translations.idiomatic: [1] Yang Bing then indicted Hou Lan and the eunuch Ju Yuan:

translations.literal: Your subject examines state's old canons—eunuch officials' posts—originally lay in supplying service inner chambers—supervise dusk guard night—yet now vulgarly receive excessive favor—hold government grasp power.
translations.idiomatic: By statute eunuchs were meant only for inner-palace night service; today they receive outrageous favor and seize the levers of state.'

sentences.zh: 其阿諛取容者，則因公□舉，以報私惠；

translations.literal: Those flattering to win favor—then borrow public □ nomination—to repay private grace;
translations.idiomatic: Flatterers trade public office [graph missing] to repay private debts;'

sentences.zh: 有忤逆於心者，必求事中傷，肆其凶忿。

translations.literal: Those who oppose disobey in heart—surely seek affair middle wound—unleash their fierce rage.
translations.idiomatic: anyone who crosses them is framed and ruined in blind fury.'

sentences.zh: 居法王公，富擬國家，飲食極餚軹，僕妾盈紈素，雖季氏專魯，穰侯擅秦，何以尚茲！

translations.literal: Dwell law king-dukes—wealth rivals state house—drink food extreme delicacies carriage—servant concubines fill white silk—though Ji clan monopolized Lu, Marquis Rang monopolized Qin—how can exceed this!
translations.idiomatic: They live like princes, wealth rivals the treasury, feast on the rarest viands, and fill their halls with silk-clad concubines—the Ji clan of Lu and Marquis Rang of Qin never matched this excess!'

sentences.zh: [二]案中常侍侯覽弟參，貪殘元惡，自取禍滅，覽顧知釁重，必有自疑之意，臣愚以為不宜復見親近。

translations.literal: [Two] Investigating Palace Attendant Hou Lan's younger brother Can—greedy cruel chief evil—self brought calamity extinction—Lan looks knows fault heavy—surely has self-doubt intent—your subject foolishly deems not fitting again see intimate near.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Can was Hou Lan's creature and chief villain—his suicide proves the guilt is grave; Hou Lan must know he is suspect. I beg you not to keep such men close.'

sentences.zh: 昔懿公刑邴歜之父，奪閻職之妻，而使二人參乘，卒有竹中之難，春秋書之，以為至戒。

translations.literal: Formerly Duke Yi executed Bing Chou's father, seized Yan Zhi's wife—yet made two men ride third seat—finally had bamboo-middle disaster—Spring and Autumn records it—took as utmost warning.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Yi of Qi mutilated Bing Chou's father, seized Yan Zhi's wife, yet made both ride in his chariot—and died in the bamboo grove; the Annals record it as the gravest warning.'

sentences.zh: [三]蓋鄭詹來而國亂，四佞放而觿服。

translations.literal: [Three] Cover Zhan of Zheng came yet state chaos—four flatterers banished yet xi submitted.
translations.idiomatic: [3] Zhan of Zheng brought chaos; when the Four Fiends were cast out the realm submitted.'

sentences.zh: [四]以此觀之，容可近乎？

translations.literal: [Four] By this viewing it—may it be drawn near?
translations.idiomatic: [4] Judging by this—can he be kept near?'

sentences.zh: 覽宜急屏斥，投畀*

translations.literal: Lan ought urgently screen expel—cast to *
translations.idiomatic: Banish Hou Lan at once and cast him out among jackals and tigers, as the Odes bid when ridding the realm of the wicked.'

translations.idiomatic: (variant manuscript marker)

translations.literal: **[Jackals]* tigers.

translations.literal: [Five] Such as these men—not grace what pardons—request dismiss office send return native commandery.
translations.idiomatic: [5] Men like this deserve no indulgence—strip their offices and send them home.'

sentences.zh: 」書奏，尚書召對秉掾屬曰：[六]「公府外職，而奏劾近官，經典漢制有故事乎？

translations.idiomatic: ' When the memorial went up, the Masters of Writing challenged Yang Bing's staff: [6] 'The Excellencies are outside posts—do the classics or Han precedent allow impeaching inner courtiers?'

sentences.zh: 」秉使對曰：「春秋趙鞅以晉陽之甲，逐君側之惡。

translations.literal: ' Bing ordered reply: 'Spring and Autumn Zhao Yang with Jinyang's armor—expelled ruler-side evil.
translations.idiomatic: ' Yang Bing's men answered: the Annals praise Zhao Yang of Jin for marching from Jinyang to purge evil beside the throne.'

sentences.zh: [七]傳曰：『除君之惡，唯力是視。

translations.literal: [Seven] Tradition says: 'Remove ruler's evil—only strength is regarded.
translations.idiomatic: [7] The tradition says: extirpate evil for the ruler with every ounce of strength.'

sentences.zh: 』[八]鄧通懈慢，申屠嘉召通詰責，文帝從而請之。

translations.idiomatic: ' [8] When Deng Tong slighted court ritual, Chancellor Shen Tu Jia summoned and rebuked him, and Emperor Wen intervened on Deng's behalf.'

sentences.zh: [九]漢世故事，三公之職無所不統。

translations.literal: [Nine] Han age precedents—Three Excellencies' duties nothing not oversee.
translations.idiomatic: [9] Han precedent gives the Three Excellencies authority over all affairs.'

sentences.zh: 尚書不能詰。

translations.literal: Masters of Writing could not challenge.
translations.idiomatic: The Masters of Writing had no reply.

sentences.zh: 帝不得已，竟免覽官，而削瑗國。

translations.literal: Emperor had no choice—in the end dismissed Lan's office—yet shaved Yuan's state.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor had to dismiss Hou Lan and strip Ju Yuan's fief.

sentences.zh: 每朝廷有得失，輒盡忠規諫，多見納用。

translations.literal: Whenever court had gain loss—then exhaust loyal remonstrate—many seen accepted employed.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever policy faltered he remonstrated to the limit, and much of his counsel was adopted.

sentences.zh: 注[一]謝承書曰：「秉奏『參取受罪臧累億。

translations.literal: [One] Xie Cheng's book says: 'Bing memorialized 'Can took received crime bribery piled hundred million.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Xie Cheng records Yang's memorial: 'Can extorted bribes worth hundreds of millions—'

sentences.zh: 牂柯男子張攸，居為富室，參橫加非罪，雲造訛言，殺攸家八人，沒入廬宅。

translations.literal: Zangke man Zhang You—dwelling was rich house—Can sideways added false crime—claimed fabricated false words—killed You household eight people—confiscated entered huts dwellings.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang You of Zangke was a wealthy commoner; Can trumped up charges, called it slander, murdered eight of his family, and seized his property.'

sentences.zh: 又與同郡諸生李元之官，共飲酒，醉飽之後，戲故相犯，誣言有淫慝之罪，應時捶殺。

translations.literal: Also with same commandery various students Li Yuan's office—together drank wine—after drunk full—play old mutually offend—falsely claimed had lewd evil crime—responded time beat killed.
translations.idiomatic: With fellow townsman Li Yuan he drank, then on a jest falsely accused him of debauchery and beat him to death at once.'

sentences.zh: 以人臣之勢，行桀紂之態，傷和逆理，痛感天地，宜當愨持，以謝一州』。

translations.literal: By human minister's power—acted Jie-Zhou's manner—harmed harmony reversed pattern—moved grief Heaven Earth—ought ought sincere hold—to apologize one province.'
translations.idiomatic: Wielding a minister's power like Jie or Zhou, he violated nature and outraged Heaven—he must be seized to appease the province.'"

sentences.zh: 又曰『京兆尹袁逢於長安客舍中得參重車三百餘乘，金銀珍玩，不可稱記。

translations.literal: Also said 'Jingzhao Governor Yuan Feng at Chang'an guest lodging obtained Can heavy carts three hundred-odd vehicles—gold silver precious curios—cannot be reckoned recorded.
translations.idiomatic: It added that Governor Yuan Feng of Jingzhao found more than three hundred loaded carts of gold, silver, and treasure in a Chang'an inn—beyond counting.'"

sentences.zh: 』」注[二]季氏，魯卿，世專魯政。

translations.literal: '" [Two] Ji clan—Lu minister—generations monopolized Lu government.
translations.idiomatic: '" [2] The Ji were Lu ministers who seized hereditary control.'"

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「季氏富於周公。

translations.literal: Confucius said: 'Ji clan richer than Duke of Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said the Ji were richer than the Duke of Zhou.'

sentences.zh: 」史記曰，穰侯魏冉者，秦昭王母宣太后弟也，為秦相國，侈富於王室。

translations.literal: ' Shiji says: Marquis Rang Wei Ran—was Qin King Zhao's mother Empress Dowager Xuan's younger brother—was Qin Chancellor—extravagant rich beyond royal house.
translations.idiomatic: ' The Shiji says Marquis Rang, the queen mother's brother, served as Qin chancellor and outspent the royal house.'"

sentences.zh: 尚猶加也。

translations.literal: Shang still means exceed.
translations.idiomatic: Shang here means 'still more' or 'surpass.'

sentences.zh: 注[四]公羊傳曰：「鄭詹自齊逃來，何以書？

translations.literal: [Four] Gongyang says: 'Zhan of Zheng from Qi fled came—why is it written?
translations.idiomatic: [4] The Gongyang asks why the flight of Zheng Zhan from Qi is recorded.'

sentences.zh: 甚佞也，曰佞人來矣。

translations.literal: Extremely flattering—says flatterer has come.
translations.idiomatic: Because a thoroughgoing flatterer had arrived.'"

sentences.zh: 」後魯莊公取齊淫女，卒為後敗。

translations.literal: ' Later Lu Duke Zhuang took Qi licentious woman—in the end by empress defeated.
translations.idiomatic: ' Later Duke Zhuang of Lu married a debauched woman of Qi and was ruined by her.'

sentences.zh: 四佞即四凶也。

translations.literal: Four flatterers are the Four Fiends.
translations.idiomatic: The four flatterers are the same as the Four Fiends of old.'

sentences.zh: 注[五]畀，與也。

translations.literal: [Five] Bi means give.
translations.idiomatic: [5] Bi means 'to cast among' or 'to give over.'

sentences.zh: 詩小雅曰：「取彼譖人，投畀豺虎。

translations.literal: Lesser Ya says: 'Seize those slanderers—cast them to jackals tigers.
translations.idiomatic: The Lesser Ya says: seize slanderers and throw them to jackals and tigers.'

sentences.zh: 」 注[六]召秉掾屬問之。

translations.literal: ' [Six] Summoned Bing's clerk staff questioned them.
translations.idiomatic: ' [6] The court summoned Yang Bing's staff for questioning.'

sentences.zh: 注[七]公羊傳曰：「趙鞅取晉陽之甲，以逐荀寅、士吉射。

translations.literal: [Seven] Gongyang says: 'Zhao Yang took Jinyang's armor—to expel Xun Yin, Shi Jishe.
translations.idiomatic: [7] The Gongyang records Zhao Yang leading armed men from Jinyang to expel Xun Yin and Shi Jishe.'

sentences.zh: 曷為此？

translations.literal: Why this?
translations.idiomatic: Why did he do this?'

sentences.zh: 逐君側之惡人也。」

translations.literal: To expel ruler-side evil men.'"
translations.idiomatic: To purge evil men from the ruler's side.'"

sentences.zh: 注[八]左傳曰晉寺人披言也。

translations.literal: [Eight] Zuo Commentary says—Jin eunuch Pi's words.
translations.idiomatic: [8] A line from the eunuch Pi of Jin in the Zuo Commentary.'

sentences.zh: 注[九]前書鄧通，文帝幸臣，為太中大夫，居上傍怠慢。

translations.literal: [Nine] Former Book Deng Tong—Wendi favored minister—was Grand Household Grandee—dwelt beside superior negligent slack.
translations.idiomatic: [9] Deng Tong, Emperor Wen's favorite, served as grand household grandee and behaved disrespectfully at court.'

sentences.zh: 丞相申屠嘉罷朝，坐府中，召通至，不為禮，責曰：「通小臣，戲殿上，大不敬，當斬。

translations.literal: Chancellor Shen Tu Jia dismissed court—sat in office—summoned Tong arrived—did not perform rites—blamed saying: 'Tong petty minister—plays on hall—great irreverence—ought behead.
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor Shen Tu Jia, after court, summoned Deng Tong to his yamen, refused courtesy, and said: 'You are a petty minister who frolics in the hall—capital crime.'

sentences.zh: 」通頓首，首盡出血。

translations.literal: ' Tong knocked head—head exhausted bleeding out.
translations.idiomatic: ' Deng Tong knocked his head on the ground until it bled.'

sentences.zh: 上使使持節召通而謝丞相：「此吾弄臣，君釋之。」

translations.literal: Superior sent envoy bearing tally summon Tong and apologize to Chancellor: 'This is Our play minister—lord release him.'"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor sent an envoy with tally to summon Deng and apologized to the chancellor: 'He is my jester—release him.'"

sentences.zh: 秉性不飲酒，又早喪夫人，遂不復娶，所在以淳白稱。

translations.literal: Bing by nature did not drink—also early lost wife—thereupon not again marry—wherever dwelt by pure white praised.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Bing drank no wine, lost his wife young, never remarried, and everywhere was praised for plain integrity.

sentences.zh: 嘗從容言曰：「我有三不惑：酒，色，財也。

translations.literal: Once leisurely said: 'I have three non-bewilderments: wine, sex, wealth.
translations.idiomatic: He once remarked calmly: 'Three things never tempt me—wine, lust, and money.'

sentences.zh: 」八年薨，時年七十四，賜塋陪陵。

translations.literal: ' Eighth year died—aged seventy-four—granted tomb accompany mausoleum.
translations.idiomatic: ' He died in the eighth year of his tenure, aged seventy-four, with a tomb granted beside the imperial mausoleum.'

sentences.zh: 子賜。

translations.literal: Son Ci.
translations.idiomatic: His son was Yang Ci.

sentences.zh: 秉子賜

translations.literal: Bing's son Ci
translations.idiomatic: Yang Ci, son of Yang Bing

sentences.zh: 賜字伯獻。

translations.literal: Ci, courtesy Boxian.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Ci, courtesy Boxian.

sentences.zh: 少傳家學，篤志博聞。

translations.literal: Youth transmitted household learning—steadfast aim broad hearing.
translations.idiomatic: From youth he inherited the family scholarship, devoted himself to study, and read widely.

sentences.zh: 常退居隱約，教授門徒，不荅州郡禮命。

translations.literal: Often withdrew dwelling hidden restrained—taught disciples—did not answer province commandery courtesy summons.
translations.idiomatic: He lived quietly, taught students, and ignored provincial appointments.

sentences.zh: 後辟大將軍梁冀府，非其好也。

translations.literal: Later recruited Grand General Liang Ji mansion—not his liking.
translations.idiomatic: Later he was called to Grand General Liang Ji's staff—work he disliked.

sentences.zh: 出除陳倉令，因病不行。

translations.literal: Went out appointed Cangcang Magistrate—by illness did not go.
translations.idiomatic: He was named magistrate of Cangcang but never took office because of illness.

sentences.zh: 公車徵不至，連辭三公之命。

translations.literal: Imperial Carriage summon not arrived—in succession declined Three Excellencies' orders.
translations.idiomatic: Imperial summons failed to bring him; he repeatedly refused the Three Excellencies.

sentences.zh: 後以司空高第，再遷侍中、越騎校尉。

translations.literal: Later by Minister of Works high rank—twice transferred Palace Attendant, Colonel of Agile Cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: On the strength of top scores under the Minister of Works he rose twice to palace attendant and colonel of agile cavalry.

sentences.zh: 建寧初，靈帝當受學，詔太傅、三公選通尚書桓君章句宿有重名者，三公舉賜，乃侍講於華光殿中。

translations.literal: At Jianning's start, Emperor Ling about to receive learning—edicted Grand Tutor, Three Excellencies select penetrate Documents Huan's lord chapter-sentence long having heavy name—Three Excellencies nominated Ci—then attendant lectured in Huaguang Hall.
translations.idiomatic: Early in Jianning, when Emperor Ling began his classical studies, the court ordered the grand tutor and Three Excellencies to choose a noted scholar of the Documents in Huan's tradition; they nominated Yang Ci, who lectured in the Huaguang Hall.

sentences.zh: [一]遷少府、光祿勳。

translations.literal: [One] transferred Minister Steward, Household Grandee.
translations.idiomatic: [1] He was promoted to minister steward and household grandee.

sentences.zh: 注[一]洛陽宮殿名曰：「華光殿在崇光殿北。」

translations.literal: [One] Luoyang Palace Halls Record says: 'Huaguang Hall is north of Chongguang Hall.'"
translations.idiomatic: [1] The Luoyang palace gazetteer places Huaguang Hall north of Chongguang Hall.'"

sentences.zh: 熹平元年，青憨見御坐，帝以問賜，賜上封事曰：

translations.literal: Xiping first year, green han appeared on imperial seat—emperor thereby asked Ci—Ci submitted sealed document saying:
translations.idiomatic: In Xiping 1 a green snake appeared on the throne; the emperor asked Yang Ci, who submitted a sealed memorial:

sentences.zh: 臣聞和氣致祥，乖氣致□，休征則五福應，[一]咎徵則六極至。

translations.literal: Your subject has heard harmonizing qi brings auspice—discordant qi brings □—auspicious signs then five blessings respond; [one] fault signs then six extremes arrive.
translations.idiomatic: I have read that harmony brings blessing and discord brings prodigy [graph missing]; good omens summon the five blessings [1], ill omens bring the six extremes.'

sentences.zh: [二]夫善不妄來，□不空發。

translations.literal: [Two] As for good not vainly comes—□ not empty issues.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Blessing never comes without cause, nor calamity without reason [graph missing].'

sentences.zh: 王者心有所惟，意有所想，雖未形顏色，而五星以之推移，陰陽為其變度。

translations.literal: King's mind has what it ponders—thought has what it pictures—though not yet formed in face color—yet five planets by it shift move—yin yang for it change measure.
translations.idiomatic: What the ruler harbors in mind moves the five planets and shifts yin and yang before his face shows it.'

sentences.zh: 以此而觀，天之與人，豈不符哉？

translations.literal: By this viewing—Heaven's with man—how could it not match!
translations.idiomatic: From this we see Heaven and man are not unaligned.'

sentences.zh: 尚書曰：『天齊乎人，假我一日。

translations.literal: The Documents says: 'Heaven aligns with man—lend me one day.
translations.idiomatic: The Documents says Heaven aligns with mankind and grants the ruler his day.'

sentences.zh: 』是其明征也。

translations.literal: ' This is its clear verification.
translations.idiomatic: ' That is plain proof.'

sentences.zh: [三]夫皇極不建，則有蛇龍之孽。

translations.literal: [Three] As for supreme pole not established—then have snake-dragon prodigies.
translations.idiomatic: [3] When the great norm is not upheld, snake and dragon portents appear.'

sentences.zh: [四]詩云：『惟虺惟蛇，女子之祥。

translations.literal: [Four] Odes say: 'Only lizards only snakes—women's omens.
translations.idiomatic: [4] The Odes say: only snakes and lizards—omens of a woman's power.'

sentences.zh: 』[五]故春秋兩蛇□於鄭門，昭公殆以女敗；

translations.literal: ' [Five] Hence Spring and Autumn two snakes □ at Zheng gate—Duke Zhao probably by woman defeated;
translations.idiomatic: ' [5] The Annals record two snakes [action missing] at Zheng's gate—Duke Zhao nearly fell to a woman's intrigue;'

sentences.zh: [六]康王一朝晏起，關睢見幾而作。

translations.literal: [Six] King Kang one morning late rose—Guan sui saw turning point then acted.
translations.idiomatic: [6] King Kang of Zhou rose late one morning, and the 'Guanju' ode warned him at the first sign.'

sentences.zh: [七]夫女謁行則讒夫昌，讒夫昌則苞苴通，故殷湯以之自戒，終濟亢旱之□。

translations.literal: [Seven] As for women's petitions move then slander men flourish—slander men flourish then gift bundles pass—hence Yin Tang by it self warned—finally crossed drought's □.
translations.idiomatic: [7] When women peddle influence, slander thrives, bribes flow, and even Tang of Yin took warning—thus he overcame great drought [graph missing].'

sentences.zh: [八]惟陛下思干剛之道，別內外之宜，崇帝乙之制，受元吉之祉，[九]抑皇甫之權，割艷妻之愛，[一０]則蛇變可消，禎祥立應。

translations.literal: [Eight] Only may Your Majesty ponder Qian firm way—separate inner outer's fitness—revere Emperor Yi's system—receive utmost auspice's blessing; [nine] suppress Huangfu's power—cut dazzling wife's love; [ten] then snake change can vanish—auspicious signs immediately respond.
translations.idiomatic: [8] Ponder the firm virtue of Qian, separate inner from outer court, honor Emperor Yi's marriage rites, embrace the great blessing; [9] curb the Huangfu faction, [10] set aside infatuation with a favorite—then the snake omen will lift and good fortune return at once.'

sentences.zh: 殷戊、宋景，其事甚明。

translations.literal: Yin Wu, Song Jing—their affairs very clear.
translations.idiomatic: The cases of King Taiwu of Yin and Duke Jing of Song make this plain.'

sentences.zh: [一一]

translations.literal: [Eleven]
translations.idiomatic: [11] (note marker)

sentences.zh: 注[一]休，美也。

translations.literal: [One] Xiu means beautiful.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Xiu means auspicious or favorable.'

sentences.zh: 征，驗也。

translations.literal: Zheng means verification.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng means a verified sign.'

sentences.zh: 五福：一曰壽，二曰富，三曰康寧，四曰逌好德，五曰考終命。

translations.literal: Five blessings: first called long life, second called wealth, third called peace and health, fourth called ease in loving virtue, fifth called completing life's span.
translations.idiomatic: The five blessings are long life, wealth, health, love of virtue, and a peaceful end.'

sentences.zh: 注[二]咎，惡也。

translations.literal: [Two] Jiu means evil.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Jiu means calamity or fault.'

sentences.zh: 六極：一曰凶短折，二曰疾，三曰憂，四曰貧，五曰惡，六曰弱。

translations.literal: Six extremes: first called violent early death, second called sickness, third called sorrow, fourth called poverty, fifth called ugliness, sixth called weakness.
translations.idiomatic: The six extremes are premature death, disease, grief, poverty, wickedness, and frailty.'

sentences.zh: 並見尚書。

translations.literal: Both seen in Documents.
translations.idiomatic: Both lists appear in the Book of Documents.'

sentences.zh: 注[三]我謂君也。

translations.literal: [Three] Wo means ruler.
translations.idiomatic: [3] 'We' here means the ruler.'

sentences.zh: 天意欲整齊於人，必假於君也。

translations.literal: Heaven's intent wishing to order-align with man—must borrow from ruler.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven orders mankind through the sovereign.'

sentences.zh: 今尚書文「假」作「俾」。

translations.literal: Now Documents text 'jia' written 'bi'.
translations.idiomatic: Some recensions write bi instead of jia.'

sentences.zh: 俾，使也。

translations.literal: Bi means send.
translations.idiomatic: Bi means 'to commission.'

sentences.zh: 義亦通。

translations.literal: Meaning also connects.
translations.idiomatic: The sense still holds.'

sentences.zh: 注[四]洪範五行傳曰。

translations.literal: [Four] Hong Fan Five Phases Treatise says.
translations.idiomatic: [4] From the Hong Fan Wuxing zhuan.'

sentences.zh: 皇，大也。

translations.literal: Huang means great.
translations.idiomatic: Huang means 'great.'

sentences.zh: 極，中也。

translations.literal: Ji means center.
translations.idiomatic: Ji means the central norm.'

sentences.zh: 建，立也。

translations.literal: Jian means establish.
translations.idiomatic: Jian means 'to establish.'

sentences.zh: 孽，□也。

translations.literal: Nie, □ also.
translations.idiomatic: Nie denotes a prodigious misgrowth [graph missing in source].'

sentences.zh: 君不合大中，是謂不立。

translations.literal: Ruler not matching great center—this called not established.
translations.idiomatic: When the ruler misses the great mean, the norm is said to be 'unestablished.'

sentences.zh: 蛇龍，陰類也。

translations.literal: Snake-dragon—yin category.
translations.idiomatic: Snakes and dragons belong to the yin category of omens.'

sentences.zh: 注[五]詩小雅也。

translations.literal: [Five] Lesser Ya.
translations.idiomatic: [5] From the Lesser Ya.'

sentences.zh: 虺蛇，穴居，陰之類，故為女子之祥也。

translations.literal: Lizards snakes—hole dwell—yin's category—therefore are women's omens.
translations.idiomatic: Burrowing snakes are creatures of yin—hence omens of women's power.'

sentences.zh: 注[六]洪範五行傳曰：「初，鄭厲公劫相祭仲而篡兄昭公，立為鄭君。

translations.literal: [Six] Hong Fan Five Phases Treatise says: 'At first, Zheng Duke Li coerced Chancellor Zhong Zhong and usurped elder brother Duke Zhao—established as Zheng lord.
translations.idiomatic: [6] The Hong Fan Wuxing zhuan: Duke Li of Zheng seized Chancellor Zhong and supplanted Duke Zhao—'

sentences.zh: 後雍愨之難，厲公出奔，鄭人立昭公。

translations.literal: Later Yongque's disaster—Li duke fled—Zheng men established Duke Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: After the Yongque crisis Li fled and the people enthroned Zhao.'

sentences.zh: 既立，內蛇與外蛇□鄭南門中。

translations.literal: Once established—inner snake with outer snake □ in Zheng south gate center.
translations.idiomatic: Once enthroned, inner and outer snakes [action missing] at Zheng's south gate.'

sentences.zh: 內蛇死。

translations.literal: Inner snake died.
translations.idiomatic: The inner snake died.'

sentences.zh: 是時傅瑕仕於鄭，欲內厲公，故內蛇死者，昭公將敗，厲公將勝之象也。

translations.literal: At this time Fu Xia served in Zheng—wished bring in Li duke—hence inner snake death—Duke Zhao about to defeat—Li duke about to victory's image.
translations.idiomatic: Fu Xia then schemed to restore Li; the dead inner snake foretold Zhao's fall and Li's triumph.'

sentences.zh: 是時昭公宜布恩施惠，以撫百姓，舉賢崇德，以厲腢臣，觀察左右，以省奸謀，則內變不得生，外謀無由起矣。

translations.literal: At this time Duke Zhao ought spread grace show favor—to soothe hundred surnames—raise worthy revere virtue—to sharpen ministers—observe inspect left right—to examine crafty plots—then inner change cannot arise—outer plots no cause rise.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao should have spread kindness, promoted worth, stiffened his ministers, and watched his flanks—then no coup could form within or intrigue without.'

sentences.zh: 昭公不覺，果殺於傅瑕，二子死而厲公入，此其效也。

translations.literal: Duke Zhao did not perceive—indeed killed by Fu Xia—two sons died yet Li duke entered—this its verification.
translations.idiomatic: He did not; Fu Xia killed him, both princes died, and Li returned—proof of the omen.'

sentences.zh: 詩云：『惟虺惟蛇，女子之祥。

translations.literal: Odes say: 'Only lizards only snakes—women's omens.
translations.idiomatic: The ode says: only snakes—omens of a woman's rise.'

sentences.zh: 』鄭昭公殆以女子敗矣。」

translations.literal: ' Duke Zhao of Zheng probably by woman defeated.'"
translations.idiomatic: So Duke Zhao of Zheng fell to a woman's faction.'"

sentences.zh: 注[七]前書曰：「佩玉晏鳴，關睢歎之。

translations.literal: [Seven] Former Book says: 'Jade pendants late crow—Guan sui sighs it.
translations.idiomatic: [7] The Han shu: late jade chimes at cockcrow—'Guanju' laments it.'

sentences.zh: 」音義曰：「後夫人，雞鳴佩玉去君所。

translations.literal: ' Sounds meanings says: 'Empress consort—cockcrow jade pendants go to lord's place.
translations.idiomatic: ' The glossary notes the queen should leave for court at cockcrow with jade sounding.'

sentences.zh: 周康王后不然，故詩人歎而傷之。

translations.literal: Zhou Kang Wang's empress not so—therefore poet sighed and wounded it.
translations.idiomatic: Queen Kang of Zhou did not—hence the poet's lament.'

sentences.zh: 此事見魯詩，今亡失也。」

translations.literal: This affair seen in Lu Odes—now lost gone.'"
translations.idiomatic: The story survives only in the Lu school text, now lost.'"

sentences.zh: 注[八]說宛曰：「湯自伐桀後，大旱七年，洛川竭，使人持三足鼎祝於山川曰：『政不節邪？

translations.literal: [Eight] Shuoyuan says: 'Tang after attacking Jie—great drought seven years—Luo rivers exhausted—sent men grasping three-legged cauldron prayed at mountains rivers saying: 'Government not restrained evil?
translations.idiomatic: [8] The Shuoyuan: after Tang conquered Jie, seven years of drought dried the Luo; he sent men with a tripod to pray: 'Is my government lax?'

sentences.zh: 使人疾邪？

translations.literal: Make people sick evil?
translations.idiomatic: Do the people suffer?'

sentences.zh: 苞苴行邪？

translations.literal: Gift bundles go evil?
translations.idiomatic: Do bribes run unchecked?'

sentences.zh: 讒夫昌邪？

translations.literal: Slander men flourish evil?
translations.idiomatic: Do slanderers thrive?'

sentences.zh: 宮室榮邪？

translations.literal: Palace chambers glory evil?
translations.idiomatic: Are palaces too lavish?'

sentences.zh: 女謁行邪？

translations.literal: Women's petitions go evil?
translations.idiomatic: Do women meddle in state affairs?'

sentences.zh: 何不雨之極！

translations.literal: Why not rain's utmost!
translations.idiomatic: Why will you not send rain?'

sentences.zh: 』言未已而天大雨。」

translations.literal: ' Words not yet finished yet Heaven great rain.'"
translations.idiomatic: ' Before he finished, Heaven sent a downpour.'"

sentences.zh: 注[九]易泰卦六五曰「帝乙歸妹，以祉元吉」也。
