translations.literal: How?
translations.idiomatic: Why was that?'

sentences.zh: 」曰：「文王之囿方七十里，芻蕘者往焉，雉菟者往焉，與人同之，人以為小，不亦宜乎？

translations.literal: ' Answered saying: 'King Wen's park square seventy li—grass cutters went there—pheasant rabbit catchers went there—with people shared it—people thought small—not also fitting?
translations.idiomatic: The reply: King Wen shared his seventy li with woodcutters and hunters; because it was open to all, it seemed small—was that not fitting?'

sentences.zh: 」此雲文王百里，齊宣五里，與孟子不同也。

translations.literal: ' Here says King Wen hundred li—Qi Xuan five li—with Mencius not same.
translations.idiomatic: The passage above gives King Wen a hundred li and King Xuan of Qi five li, which differs from Mencius's figures.'

sentences.zh: 四年，賜以病罷。

translations.literal: Fourth year—Ci because illness dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year Yang Ci resigned on grounds of illness.

sentences.zh: 居無何，拜太常，詔賜御府衣一襲，[一]自所服冠幘綬，玉壺革帶，金錯鉤佩。

translations.literal: Dwelt not long—appointed Grand Master of Ceremonies—edict granted Ci imperial wardrobe clothes one set; [one] personally worn cap headband ribbon—jade pot leather belt—gold inlay hook pendant.
translations.idiomatic: Before long he was appointed Grand Master of Ceremonies; an edict gave him a full set of robes from the imperial wardrobe [1], the cap, kerchief, and ribbons he had worn himself, a jade flask, a leather belt, and gold-inlaid belt hooks and pendants.

translations.literal: Second note.
translations.idiomatic: This sentence marks the second commentary note in sequence.

sentences.zh: 注[一]衣單復具曰襲。

translations.literal: [One] Clothes single layered double layered complete called set.
translations.idiomatic: [1] A complete outfit of inner and outer garments is called a xi (set).

sentences.zh: 注[二]金錯，以金閒錯其文。

translations.literal: [Two] Gold inlay—with gold interspersed inlay its patterns.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Gold inlay (jin cuo) means patterns worked with gold set into the metal.

sentences.zh: 五年冬，復拜太尉。

translations.literal: Fifth year winter—again appointed Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In winter of the fifth year he was again appointed Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 中平元年，黃巾賊起，賜被召會議詣省合，切諫忤旨，因以寇賊免。

translations.literal: Zhongping first year—Yellow Turban bandits arose—Ci summoned meet council arrive province compound—sharp remonstrance oppose intent—thereby because bandit rebels dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In Zhongping 1 the Yellow Turbans rose; Yang Ci was summoned to a council at the secretariat, spoke bluntly against policy, offended the throne, and was removed on the pretext of the rebellion.

sentences.zh: 先是黃巾帥張角等執左道，稱大賢，以誑耀百姓，天下襁負歸之。

translations.literal: Before this Yellow Turban commander Zhang Jue and others held heterodox ways—called great worthies—to deceive dazzle hundred surnames—all under heaven swaddled on backs returned to them.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier, Zhang Jue and other Turban leaders had preached deviant doctrines, styled themselves great sages, dazzled the common folk, and people from every quarter flocked to them with infants on their backs.

sentences.zh: 賜時在司徒，召掾劉陶告曰：「張角等遭赦不悔，而稍益滋蔓，今若下州郡捕討，恐更騷擾，速成其患。

translations.literal: Ci at time was Minister of Education—summoned clerk Liu Tao told saying: 'Zhang Jue and others met amnesty not repent—yet gradually more spread vine—now if send down provinces commanderies capture attack—fear moreover disturb—quickly complete their calamity.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Ci was Minister of Education; he called in his clerk Liu Tao and said, 'Zhang Jue and his followers were pardoned yet show no remorse; their movement only spreads. If we now order every province and commandery to hunt them down, we may stir greater unrest and hasten the disaster.'

sentences.zh: 且欲切□刺史﹑二千石，簡別流人，各護歸本郡，以孤弱其黨，然後誅其渠帥，可不勞而定，何如？

translations.literal: Moreover wish cut (text missing) Inspectors, two-thousand-dan—sort separate drifting people—each escort return original commanderies—to isolate weaken their faction—thereafter execute their channel leaders—can without toil and settle—how?
translations.idiomatic: I would rather sternly admonish the provincial governors and two-thousand-dan officials, sort the displaced, escort each group home to its native commandery to isolate and weaken the sect, then strike the ringleaders—could we not settle this with less turmoil? What do you think?'

sentences.zh: 」陶對曰：「此孫子所謂不戰而屈人之兵，廟勝之術也。

translations.literal: ' Liu Tao answered saying: 'This Sunzi's so-called not battle yet bend others' troops—temple victory's technique.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Tao replied, 'That is what Sunzi meant by subduing the enemy without fighting—the art of winning in the ancestral temple before the campaign.'

sentences.zh: 」[一]賜遂上書言之。

translations.literal: ' [One] Ci thereupon ascended memorial spoke it.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Yang Ci then submitted a memorial explaining the plan.

sentences.zh: 會去位，事留中。

translations.literal: Met left position—matters detained within.
translations.idiomatic: Before long he left office, and the proposal was shelved inside the palace.

sentences.zh: [二]後帝徙南宮，閱錄故事，得賜所上張角奏及前侍講注籍，[三]乃感悟，下詔封賜臨晉侯，邑千五百戶。

translations.literal: [Two] Later emperor moved Southern Palace—reviewed recorded precedents—obtained Ci's submitted Zhang Jue memorial and former lecture-attendant annotated registers; [three] then comprehended moved—issued edict enfeoffed Ci Linjin marquis—estate thousand five hundred households.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Later the emperor moved to the Southern Palace, reviewed old files, and found Yang Ci's memorial on Zhang Jue and the annotated lecture rolls from his days as instructor [3]. Moved, he issued an edict enfeoffing Yang Ci as marquis of Linjin with fifteen hundred households.

sentences.zh: [四]初，賜與太尉劉寬﹑司空張濟[五]並入侍講，自以不宜獨受封賞，上書願分戶邑於寬﹑濟。

translations.literal: [Four] At first—Ci with Grand Commandant Liu Kuan, Minister of Works Zhang Ji [five] together entered attendant lecture—self thought not fitting alone receive enfeoffment rewards—ascended memorial wished divide household estate to Kuan, Ji.
translations.idiomatic: [4] Earlier, Yang Ci had joined the lecture corps with Grand Commandant Liu Kuan and Minister of Works Zhang Ji [5]. Believing he should not keep the reward alone, he petitioned to share the fief with Kuan and Ji.

sentences.zh: 帝嘉歎，復封寬及濟子，拜賜尚書令。

translations.literal: Emperor praised sighed—again enfeoffed Kuan and Ji's son—appointed Ci Director of the Secretariat.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor praised his generosity, enfeoffed Liu Kuan and Zhang Ji's son as well, and appointed Yang Ci Director of the Secretariat.

sentences.zh: 數日出為廷尉，賜自以代非法家，言曰：「三後成功，惟殷於民，戲陶不與焉，蓋吝之也。

translations.literal: Several days out became Commandant of Justice—Ci self thought replace not legalist family—spoke saying: 'Three lords accomplished merit—only abundant to people—Xi Tao not join therein—covering stingy it.
translations.idiomatic: Within days he was sent out as Commandant of Justice. Yang Ci knew he was succeeding men who were not specialists in law and said, 'The Documents praises the three ministers whose merit enriched the people, yet omits Xi and Tao from the reckoning—the text is sparing them out of shame.'

sentences.zh: 」[六] 遂固辭，以特進就第。

translations.literal: ' [Six] Thereupon firmly declined—with Exceptionally Advanced took mansion.
translations.idiomatic: [6] He firmly declined and retired to private life with the rank of Exceptionally Advanced.

sentences.zh: 注[一]孫子曰：「未戰而廟勝，得筭多也。

translations.literal: [One] Sunzi said: 'Not yet battled yet temple victory—obtained calculations many.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Sunzi said: To prevail in the ancestral temple before fighting means your calculations were many.

sentences.zh: 未戰而廟不勝，得筭少也。

translations.literal: Not yet battled yet temple not victory—obtained calculations few.
translations.idiomatic: To lose in the temple before fighting means your calculations were few.

sentences.zh: 」 注[二]謂所論事留在禁中，未施用之。

translations.literal: ' [Two] Means discussed matter detained within forbidden center—not yet applied used it.
translations.idiomatic: [2] That is, the proposal was held in the inner palace and never carried out.

sentences.zh: 注[三]所注之籍錄。

translations.literal: [Three] Registered records that were annotated.
translations.idiomatic: [3] The annotated roster from those lecture sessions.

sentences.zh: 注[四]臨晉，縣，屬馮翊，故城在今同州朝邑縣西南。

translations.literal: [Four] Linjin—county—belonged Fengyi—old fort now at Tongzhou Chaoyi county southwest.
translations.idiomatic: [4] Linjin was a county in Fengyi; its old wall lay southwest of Chaoyi county in modern Tong prefecture.

sentences.zh: 注[五]濟字符江，細陽人也，張*

translations.literal: [Five] Ji styled Zijiang—Xiyang person—Zhang*
translations.idiomatic: [5] Zhang Ji, courtesy name Zijiang, was a native of Xiyang; the Zhang clan genealogy is damaged at this point in the text.

translations.literal: (Fu)
translations.idiomatic: The supplied given name is Fu (editorial restoration).

sentences.zh: *[酺]曾孫。

translations.literal: *[Pu] great-grandson.
translations.idiomatic: He was a great-grandson of Zhang Pu (emended reading).

sentences.zh: 注[六]吝，恥也。

translations.literal: [Six] Lin means shame.
translations.idiomatic: [6] Here lin means shame—the commentator implies the classic is ashamed to name them.

sentences.zh: 殷，盛也。

translations.literal: Yin means abundant.
translations.idiomatic: Yin in the line means flourishing abundance.

sentences.zh: 尚書曰：「伯夷降典，折人惟刑，禹平水土，主名山川，稷降播種，農殖嘉谷，三後成功，惟殷於人。

translations.literal: Documents says: 'Boyi descended statutes, restrained people only by punishments; Yu levelled water and earth, presided over naming mountains and rivers; Ji descended sowing planting, farming raised fine grain; three lords accomplished merit—only abundant to people.
translations.idiomatic: The Documents: Boyi gave the code and tempered the people with law; Yu tamed the floods and set the mountains and rivers in order; Ji spread sowing so the fields bore good grain—three ministers earned their merit, and the blessing was poured out on the people.

sentences.zh: 」言戲陶不預其數者，蓋恥之。

translations.literal: ' Speaking Xi Tao not participate that number—covering shame them.
translations.idiomatic: The passage omits Xi and Tao from that tally because the classic is ashamed to rank them with the three.

sentences.zh: 二年九月，復代張溫為司空。

translations.literal: Second year ninth month—again replaced Zhang Wen as Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year, ninth month, he again replaced Zhang Wen as Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 其月薨。

translations.literal: That month died.
translations.idiomatic: He died that same month.

sentences.zh: 天子素服，三日不臨朝，贈東園梓器襚服，賜錢三百萬，布五百匹。

translations.literal: Son of Heaven plain garb—three days not attend court—granted Eastern Park catalpa coffin burial clothes—granted cash three million—cloth five hundred bolts.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor wore mourning, stayed away from court for three days, sent an Eastern Park coffin and grave clothes, and granted three million cash and five hundred bolts of cloth.

sentences.zh: 策曰：

translations.literal: Edict said:
translations.idiomatic: The edict of praise read:

sentences.zh: 故司空臨晉侯賜，華岳所挺，九德純備，[一]三葉宰相，輔國以忠。

translations.literal: Late Minister of Works Linjin marquis Ci—Hua Marchmount what bore—nine virtues purely complete; [one] three generations chancellors—assist state by loyalty.
translations.idiomatic: The late Minister of Works, Marquis of Linjin Yang Ci, was nurtured by Mount Hua; the nine virtues were whole in him [1]; for three generations his house held the highest office and served the state with loyalty.

sentences.zh: 朕昔初載，授道帷幄，[二]遂階成勳，以陟大猷。

translations.literal: I former beginning carriage—received way tent curtain; [two] then stepped accomplished merit—to ascend great plan.
translations.idiomatic: In my youth he taught me within the curtain [2], helped me build merit step by step, and guided me toward the great design.

sentences.zh: 師範之功，昭於內外，庶官之務，勞亦勤止。

translations.literal: Teacher model's merit—bright within without—many offices' affairs—toil also diligent stop.
translations.idiomatic: His work as my master and model shone at court and in the realm; the burden of every ministry wore hard on him, yet he never slackened.

sentences.zh: 七在卿校，殊位特進，五登袞職，弭難乂寧。

translations.literal: Seven in ministers colonels—special rank Exceptionally Advanced—five ascended ceremonial robes office—quell disasters bring peace.
translations.idiomatic: Seven times he served among ministers and colonels; he rose to the exalted rank of Exceptionally Advanced; five times he wore the chancellor's robes and quelled crises until the realm was still.

sentences.zh: 雖受茅土，未荅厥勳，哲人其萎，將誰諮度！

translations.literal: Although received fief soil—not repaid his merit—wise man his wither—will who consult measure!
translations.idiomatic: Though enfeoffed with land, we never matched his service; now the wise man is gone—whom shall we ask to counsel the realm!

sentences.zh: 朕甚懼焉。

translations.literal: I greatly fear therein.
translations.idiomatic: I am deeply afraid of what lies ahead.

sentences.zh: [三]禮設殊等，物有服章。

translations.literal: [Three] Ritual sets special grades—things have dress insignia.
translations.idiomatic: [3] Rites set ranks apart; goods and vestments bear their proper emblems.

sentences.zh: 今使左中郎將郭儀持節追位特進，[四]贈司空驃騎將軍印綬。

translations.literal: Now ordered Left General of the Household Guo Yi hold tallies posthumously advance rank Exceptionally Advanced; [four] grant Minister of Works cavalry-general seals and ribbons.
translations.idiomatic: The court sent Left General of the Household Guo Yi with imperial tallies to posthumously advance him to Exceptionally Advanced rank and to present the seals and ribbons of Minister of Works and cavalry general.

sentences.zh: 及葬，又使侍御史持節送喪，蘭台令史十人發羽林騎輕車介士，[五]前後部鼓吹，又□驃騎將軍官屬司空法駕，送至舊塋。

translations.literal: When buried—again ordered Attending Secretary hold tallies escort mourning—Orchid Terrace clerks ten men dispatch Yulin riders light carts armored men; [five] front rear sections drum wind—again (text missing) cavalry-general subordinates Minister of Works law chariot—sent to old tomb.
translations.idiomatic: At the burial the emperor also sent an Attending Secretary with tallies to escort the cortège, ten Orchid Terrace clerks to lead Yulin guards in light war carts and armored escorts, front and rear bands of drums and pipes, and the full law chariot of a cavalry general and Minister of Works (one graph missing in the text) to the family cemetery.

sentences.zh: [六]公卿已下會葬。

translations.literal: [Six] dukes ministers down attended burial.
translations.idiomatic: Dukes, ministers, and all ranks below them attended the funeral.

sentences.zh: 謚文烈侯。

translations.literal: Posthumous title Cultured Fierce marquis.
translations.idiomatic: He was given the posthumous title Marquis Cultured and Resolute.

sentences.zh: 及小祥，又會焉。

translations.literal: Reached lesser auspicious—again assembled there.
translations.idiomatic: At the lesser auspicious anniversary of his death they gathered again.

sentences.zh: 子彪嗣。

translations.literal: Son Biao succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: His son Yang Biao inherited the title.

translations.literal: [Seven]
translations.idiomatic: This line marks the seventh commentary note in sequence.

sentences.zh: 注[一]挺，生也。

translations.literal: [One] Ting means born.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Ting here means to be born or reared (by the mountain).

sentences.zh: 九德即戲陶謨九德。

translations.literal: Nine virtues namely Yao Dian nine virtues.
translations.idiomatic: The nine virtues are those listed in the Counsels of Yao and Shun section of the Documents.

sentences.zh: 注[二]詩大雅曰：「文王初載。

translations.literal: [Two] Odes Greater Ya says: King Wen beginning zai (coming of age).
translations.idiomatic: [2] The Greater Odes line begins: In King Wen's early years (zai marks his coming of age).

sentences.zh: 」毛萇注云：「載，識也。」

translations.literal: Mao Chang commentary: zai means coming to maturity (knowing ritual and duty).
translations.idiomatic: Mao Chang glosses zai as the point when King Wen came of age.

sentences.zh: 注[三]禮記曰；

translations.literal: [Three] Record of Rites says;
translations.idiomatic: [3] The Record of Rites records the following scene.

sentences.zh: 「孔子負手曳杖，消搖於門，歌曰：『太山其頹乎，梁木其壞乎，哲人其萎乎！』」

translations.literal: Confucius with hands behind his back, trailing his staff, wandered at ease by the gate and sang that Mount Tai would fall, the great beam break, and the wise man wither.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius came to the gate with his hands behind his back, dragging his staff, and sang that Mount Tai would crumble, the great beam would break, and the wise man would fade away.

sentences.zh: 注[四]前書，張禹為丞相，以老罷就第，以列侯朝朔望，位特進，見禮如丞相。

translations.literal: [Four] Former Documents—Zhang Yu was chancellor—because old dismissed took mansion—with marquis of rank attended new-moon full-moon—rank Exceptionally Advanced—seen ritual like chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: [4] The Han shu notes that when Zhang Yu retired as chancellor he kept his marquis rank, attended court on the new and full moons, held the title Exceptionally Advanced, and was received with the ceremony due a chancellor.

sentences.zh: 漢雜事曰：「諸侯功德優盛，朝廷所敬異，賜位特進，在三公下。」

translations.literal: Han assorted affairs says that when a noble's merit was outstanding the court singled him out for the rank Exceptionally Advanced, below the Three Excellencies.
translations.idiomatic: Han assorted affairs states that when a noble's merit was outstanding the court granted him the rank of Exceptionally Advanced, immediately below the Three Excellencies.

sentences.zh: 注[五]續漢志：「輕車，古之戰車也，洞朱輪輿，不巾不蓋，菑矛戟幢麾。

translations.literal: [Five] Continued Han Treatise: 'Light cart—ancient war chariot—hollow vermillion wheel box—no curtain no cover—plant spear halberd banner pennant.
translations.idiomatic: [5] The Treatise on the Later Han: the light cart was the old war chariot, with hollow vermilion wheels, no canopy, and spears, halberds, and command banners fixed to the frame.

sentences.zh: 」菑音側事反。

translations.literal: ' Zai sound ce-shi fan.
translations.idiomatic: Read zai with the fanqie ce-shi (to insert).

sentences.zh: 菑謂插也。

translations.literal: Zai means insert.
translations.idiomatic: Zai means to set or plant the weapons upright in the cart.

sentences.zh: 注[六]續漢志「三公﹑列侯車，倚鹿，伏熊，黑轓，朱班輪，鹿文飛軨，九游降龍。

translations.literal: [Six] Continued Han Treatise 'Three Excellencies, ranked marquises' chariots—leaning deer—crouching bear—black sideboards—vermillion-spotted wheels—deer pattern flying hubs—nine streamers descending dragon.
translations.idiomatic: [6] The Treatise describes the funeral chariots of the Three Excellencies and ranked marquises: deer and bear motifs, black sideboards, vermilion-spotted wheels, deer-pattern flying hubs, and nine pennants with the descending-dragon design.

sentences.zh: 騎吏四人，皆帶□持棨戟為前列，三百石長導從，置門下五吏，賊曹﹑功曹皆帶□車道，主簿﹑主記兩車為從」也。

translations.literal: Riding clerks four men—all belt (missing) hold tally-halberds as front row—three hundred shi chief guide followers—set gate under five clerks—thieves bureau, merit bureau all belt (missing) chariot road—chief registrar, chief recorder two chariots as rear' also.
translations.idiomatic: Four mounted escorts (text damaged) carried tally halberds in the van; a chief guide of three hundred shi rank led the column; five clerks of the gate followed; the thieves and merit bureaus rode with escorts (text damaged) along the route; the chief registrar and chief recorder each had a carriage in the rear train.

sentences.zh: 注[七]禮「開而小祥」，「又開而大祥」。

translations.literal: [Seven] Rites 'opened then lesser auspicious' 'again opened then greater auspicious'.
translations.idiomatic: [7] The Rites distinguish the lesser auspicious mourning after the first lifting of mourning restrictions and the greater auspicious after the second.

sentences.zh: 鄭玄注曰：「祥，吉也，言其漸即吉也。」

translations.literal: Zheng Xuan commentary says: 'Xiang means auspicious—speaking its gradually reach auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan: xiang means auspicious—mourning gradually gives way to normal life.

sentences.zh: 賜子彪

translations.literal: Ci's son Biao
translations.idiomatic: Yang Ci's son: Yang Biao

sentences.zh: 彪字文先，少傳家學。

translations.literal: Biao styled Wenxian—youth transmitted household learning.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Biao, courtesy name Wenxian, learned his father's scholarship from boyhood.

sentences.zh: 初舉孝廉，州舉茂才，辟公府，皆不應。

translations.literal: At first recommended Filial and Incorrupt—province recommended Flourishing Talent—summoned excellencies' offices—all did not respond.
translations.idiomatic: He was first nominated Filial and Incorrupt, then the province nominated him Flourishing Talent, and he was summoned to the excellencies' bureaus, but he declined every offer.

sentences.zh: 熹平中，以博習舊聞，公車徵拜議郎，[一]遷侍中﹑京兆尹。

translations.literal: Within Xiping—because broadly practiced old lore—public carriage summoned appointed Gentleman Consultant; [one] moved Palace Attendant, Governor of the capital region.
translations.idiomatic: During the Xiping era his wide learning in classical tradition led to summons by imperial coach; he was appointed Gentleman Consultant and rose to Palace Attendant and governor of the capital region.

sentences.zh: 光和中，黃門令王甫使門生於郡界辜榷官財物七千餘萬，[二]彪發其奸，言之司隸。

translations.literal: Within Guanghe—Yellow Gate Director Wang Fu ordered disciples at commandery border monopolize extort official goods cash seventy million plus; [two] Biao exposed their wickedness—spoke to Metropolitan Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: During Guanghe the Yellow Gate director Wang Fu had his followers monopolize and squeeze official property worth more than seventy million cash in the commandery; Yang Biao exposed the fraud and reported it to the metropolitan commandant.

sentences.zh: 司隸校尉陽球因此奏誅甫，天下莫不愜心。

translations.literal: Metropolitan Commandant Yang Qiu thereby memorialized execute Fu—all under heaven none not satisfied heart.
translations.idiomatic: Metropolitan Commandant Yang Qiu memorialized for Wang Fu's execution, and people everywhere felt vindicated.

sentences.zh: 征還為侍中﹑五官中郎將，遷穎川﹑南陽太守，復拜侍中，三遷永樂少府﹑太僕﹑□尉。

translations.literal: Summoned returned became Palace Attendant, General of the Household for All Purposes—moved Yingchuan, Nanyang grand warden—again appointed Palace Attendant—three promotions Yongle privy treasurer, Coachman, (missing) commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Recalled to court he served as Palace Attendant and general of the household for all purposes, then as grand warden of Yingchuan and Nanyang, again as Palace Attendant, and after three promotions as privy treasurer of Yongle, coachman, and commandant (one character missing in the text).

sentences.zh: 注[一]華嶠書曰：「與馬日磾﹑盧植﹑蔡邕等著作東觀。

translations.literal: [One] Hua Qiao's book says: 'With Ma Midi, Lu Zhi, Cai Yong and others composed works Eastern Watchtower.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Hua Qiao's history says he worked with Ma Midi, Lu Zhi, Cai Yong, and others compiling texts in the Eastern Watchtower library.

sentences.zh: 」 注[二]華嶠書曰：「甫使門生王翹辜榷。

translations.literal: ' [Two] Hua Qiao's book says: 'Fu ordered disciple Wang Qiao monopolize extort.
translations.idiomatic: Hua Qiao also records that Wang Fu had his follower Wang Qiao run the monopoly racket.

sentences.zh: 」解見靈帝紀。

translations.literal: ' Explanation see Emperor Ling annals.
translations.idiomatic: The full account appears in the annals of Emperor Ling.

sentences.zh: 中平六年，代董卓為司空，其冬，代黃琬為司徒。

translations.literal: Zhongping sixth year—replaced Dong Zhuo as Minister of Works—that winter—replaced Huang Wan as Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: In Zhongping 6 he replaced Dong Zhuo as Minister of Works, and that winter he replaced Huang Wan as Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 明年，關東兵起，董卓懼，欲遷都以違其難。

translations.literal: Next year—east of passes troops arose—Dong Zhuo feared—wished move capital thereby avoid its hardship.
translations.idiomatic: The next year armies rose east of the passes; Dong Zhuo grew afraid and wanted to move the capital to escape the crisis.

sentences.zh: [一]乃大會公卿議曰：「高祖都關中十有一世，光武宮洛陽，於今亦十世矣。

translations.literal: [One] Thereupon greatly assembled dukes ministers deliberated saying: 'Gaozu capital Guanzhong eleven generations—Guangwu palace Luoyang—until now also ten generations.
translations.idiomatic: [1] He summoned the high ministers and said, Gaozu made Guanzhong his capital for eleven reigns; Guangwu ruled from Luoyang, and we are now in the tenth generation there.

sentences.zh: 案石包讖，宜徙都長安，以應天人之意。

translations.literal: According Stone Bundle prophecy—fitting move capital Chang'an—to respond heaven men's intent.
translations.idiomatic: The Stone Bundle prophecy says we should move the capital to Chang'an to answer the will of Heaven and men.

sentences.zh: 」百官無敢言者。

translations.literal: ' Hundred officials none dared speak ones.
translations.idiomatic: Not one of the officials dared answer.

sentences.zh: 彪曰：「移都改制，天下大事，故盤庚五遷，殷民胥怨。

translations.literal: Biao said: 'Move capital change system—all under heaven great matter—therefore Pan Geng five moves—Yin people mutually all resented.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Biao said, To move the capital and remake institutions is a matter for the whole realm; when Pan Geng moved five times, the Yin people all grumbled together.

sentences.zh: [二]*[昔]*關中遭王莽變亂，宮室焚蕩，民庶塗炭，百不一在。

translations.literal: [Two] (emendation) (emendation) Guanzhong met Wang Mang rebellion chaos—palace chambers burned swept—people masses mud charcoal—hundred not one remained.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Long ago Guanzhong suffered Wang Mang's rebellion: palaces burned, the people were ruined, and hardly one in a hundred survived.

sentences.zh: 光武受命，更都洛邑。

translations.literal: Guangwu received mandate—changed capital Luo city.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Guangwu received the mandate and moved the capital to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 今天下無虞，[三]百姓樂安，明公建立聖主，光隆漢祚，無故捐宗廟，棄園陵，恐百姓驚動，必有糜沸之亂。

translations.literal: Now all under heaven without worry; [three] hundred surnames joy peace—enlightened lord established sage ruler—bright enlarge Han fortune—without reason cast aside ancestral temples—abandon park tombs—fear hundred surnames startled move—certainly will have gruel-boil chaos.
translations.idiomatic: Today the realm is untroubled [3], the people live in peace, and you, my lord, have set up a sage Son of Heaven and enlarged Han's mandate. To abandon the ancestral shrines and imperial tombs without cause would terrify the people and bring the porridge-boiling chaos of revolt.

sentences.zh: [四]石包室讖，妖邪之書，豈可信用？」

translations.literal: [Four] Stone Bundle chamber prophecy—deviant evil's book—how can trust use?
translations.idiomatic: [4] The Stone Bundle chamber prophecy is a book of sorcery; how can we trust it?

sentences.zh: 卓曰：「關中肥饒，故秦得併吞六國。

translations.literal: Zhuo said: 'Guanzhong fat fertile—therefore Qin was able annex-swallow six states.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhuo said, Guanzhong is rich and fertile; that is how Qin swallowed the six states.

sentences.zh: 且隴右材木自出，致之甚易。

translations.literal: Moreover Longyou timber wood self produces—bring it very easy.
translations.idiomatic: Timber comes readily from Longyou, so hauling it in is easy.

sentences.zh: 又杜陵南山下有武帝故瓦陶醋數千所，並功營之，可使一朝而辨。

translations.literal: Moreover Duling south mountain under has Emperor Wu former tile pottery vinegar several thousand places—join labor build them—can make one morning and complete.
translations.idiomatic: South of Duling lie thousands of Emperor Wu's old tile-kiln and pottery works; put labor on them together and they can be ready in a single day (text may read kilns rather than vinegar).

sentences.zh: 百姓何足與議！

translations.literal: Hundred surnames what worth with deliberate!
translations.idiomatic: The commoners are not worth consulting!

sentences.zh: 若有前□，我以大兵驅之，可令詣滄海。

translations.literal: If have before (missing)—I with great army drive them—can order go to vast sea.
translations.idiomatic: If anyone stands in the way beforehand, I will drive them off with main force and send them to the eastern sea.

sentences.zh: 」[五]彪曰：「天下動之至易，安之甚難，惟明公慮焉。

translations.literal: ' [Five] Biao said: 'All under heaven move it utmost easy—pacify it utmost hard—only enlightened lord consider therein.
translations.idiomatic: [5] Yang Biao said, It is easiest to throw the realm into turmoil and hardest to settle it again; I beg you to weigh that, my lord.

sentences.zh: 」卓作色曰：「公欲沮國計邪？

translations.literal: ' Zhuo changed color said: 'Lord wishes obstruct state plan evil?
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhuo's face darkened. Do you mean to block the state's plan?

sentences.zh: 」[六]太尉黃琬曰：「此國之大事，楊公之言得無可思？

translations.literal: ' [Six] Grand Commandant Huang Wan said: 'This state's great matter—Lord Yang's words obtain nothing worth ponder?
translations.idiomatic: [6] Grand Commandant Huang Wan said, This is a great matter of state; should we not think over what Lord Yang has said?

sentences.zh: 」卓不荅。

translations.literal: ' Zhuo did not answer.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhuo made no reply.

sentences.zh: 司空荀爽見卓意壯，恐害彪等，因從容言曰：「相國豈樂此邪？

translations.literal: Minister of Works Xun Shuang saw Zhuo intent strong—feared harm Biao and others—thereby easy manner spoke saying: 'Chancellor how could enjoy this evil?
translations.idiomatic: Minister of Works Xun Shuang saw Dong Zhuo's resolve harden and feared for Yang Biao; he said lightly, Surely the chancellor takes no pleasure in this?

sentences.zh: 山東兵起，非一日可禁，故當遷以圖之，此秦﹑漢之埶也。

translations.literal: Shandong troops arose—not one day can forbid—therefore fitting move thereby plan it—this Qin, Han's situation.
translations.idiomatic: The Shandong armies will not be stopped in a day, so a move is the way to handle them; that was the logic of Qin and Han as well.

sentences.zh: 」卓意小解。

translations.literal: ' Zhuo intent slightly eased.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhuo's manner softened a little.

sentences.zh: 爽私謂彪曰：「諸君堅爭不止，禍必有歸，故吾不為也。

translations.literal: Shuang privately told Biao saying: 'Gentlemen firm argue not stop—calamity certainly will have return—therefore I not do.
translations.idiomatic: Xun Shuang said privately to Yang Biao, If you keep arguing, disaster will find its target; that is why I will not join you.

sentences.zh: 」議罷，卓使司隸校尉宣播以□異奏免琬﹑彪等，詣闕謝，即拜光祿大夫。

translations.literal: ' Council dismissed—Zhuo ordered Metropolitan Commandant Xuan Bo with (missing) strange memorialize dismiss Wan, Biao and others—went palace gate thank—thereupon appointed Grand Household Grandee.
translations.idiomatic: When the debate ended, Dong Zhuo had metropolitan commandant Xuan Bo memorialize on a pretext (text damaged) to dismiss Huang Wan, Yang Biao, and others; they went to the palace to apologize and were immediately appointed grand household grandees.

sentences.zh: 十餘日，遷大鴻臚。

translations.literal: Ten plus days—moved Grand Herald.
translations.idiomatic: After ten days or so he was promoted to grand herald.

sentences.zh: 從入關，轉少府﹑太常，以病免。

translations.literal: Followed entered passes—turned Minister Steward, Grand Master of Ceremonies—because illness dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: He followed the court into the passes, served as minister steward and grand master of ceremonies, then retired on grounds of illness.

sentences.zh: 復為京兆尹﹑光祿勳，再遷光祿大夫。

translations.literal: Again became Governor capital region, Household grandee—again moved Grand Household Grandee.
translations.idiomatic: He again became governor of the capital region and household grandee, then was promoted twice to grand household grandee.

sentences.zh: 三年秋，代淳於嘉為司空，以地震免。

translations.literal: Third year autumn—replaced Chunyu Jia as Minister of Works—because earthquake dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year, autumn, he replaced Chunyu Jia as Minister of Works but stepped down after an earthquake.

sentences.zh: 復拜太常。

translations.literal: Again appointed Grand Master of Ceremonies.
translations.idiomatic: He was again appointed grand master of ceremonies.

sentences.zh: 興平元年，代朱儁為太尉，錄尚書事。

translations.literal: Xingping first year—replaced Zhu Jun as Grand Commandant—recorded Secretariat affairs.
translations.idiomatic: In Xingping 1 he replaced Zhu Jun as grand commandant with authority over Secretariat business.

sentences.zh: 及李傕﹑郭汜之亂，彪盡節□主，崎嶇危難之閒，幾不免於害。

translations.literal: Reached Li Jue, Guo Si's chaos—Biao exhausted fidelity (missing) lord—rugged danger hardship between—almost not escaped from harm.
translations.idiomatic: During the revolt of Li Jue and Guo Si he held full loyalty to the sovereign (one graph missing) amid peril and barely escaped harm.

sentences.zh: 語在董卓傳。

translations.literal: Words in Dong Zhuo biography.
translations.idiomatic: The account is given in the biography of Dong Zhuo.

sentences.zh: 及車駕還洛陽，復守尚書令。

translations.literal: Reached imperial carriage returned Luoyang—again guarded Director of Secretariat.
translations.idiomatic: When the imperial train returned to Luoyang he again served as director of the Secretariat.

sentences.zh: 注[一]違，避也。

translations.literal: [One] Wei means avoid.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Wei here means to evade or avoid.

sentences.zh: 注[二]盤庚，殷王之名也。

translations.literal: [Two] Pan Geng—Yin king's name.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Pan Geng was the name of an Yin-dynasty king.

sentences.zh: 胥，相也。

translations.literal: Xu means together.
translations.idiomatic: Xu means all together or mutually.

sentences.zh: 遷都於亳，殷人相與怨恨。

translations.literal: Moved capital at Bo—Yin people together resented.
translations.idiomatic: When the capital was moved to Bo, the Yin people shared a common resentment.

sentences.zh: 湯遷亳，仲丁遷囂，河但甲居相，祖乙居耿，並般庚五也。

translations.literal: Tang moved Bo—Zhong Ding moved Ao—River Dan Jia dwelt Xiang—Zu Yi dwelt Geng—together with Pan Geng five.
translations.idiomatic: Tang moved to Bo, Zhong Ding to Ao, He Dan Jia (emended from He Dan Jia in some texts) to Xiang, Zu Yi to Geng—together with Pan Geng these are the five removals.

sentences.zh: 注[三]虞，度也。

translations.literal: [Three] Yu means measure.
translations.idiomatic: [3] Wu yu means there is nothing to worry about (yu: to reckon or measure).

sentences.zh: 言無可度之事也。

translations.literal: Speaks nothing can measure matters.
translations.idiomatic: That is, there is no cause for alarm.

sentences.zh: 書曰：「四方無虞。

translations.literal: Documents says: 'Four quarters without worry.
translations.idiomatic: The Documents says, The four quarters are without peril.

sentences.zh: 」 注[四]如麋粥之沸也。

translations.literal: ' [Four] Like elk gruel's boil.
translations.idiomatic: [4] Like porridge boiling over—a image of turmoil.

sentences.zh: 詩曰：「如沸如羹。」

translations.literal: Odes says: 'Like boil like gruel.
translations.idiomatic: The Odes says it will seethe like a pot and bubble like gruel.

sentences.zh: 注[五]言不敢避險難也。

translations.literal: [Five] Speaks not dare avoid danger hardship.
translations.idiomatic: [5] That is, he did not shrink from danger in speaking out.

sentences.zh: 注[六]沮，止也。

translations.literal: [Six] Ju means stop.
translations.idiomatic: [6] Ju means to obstruct or block.

sentences.zh: 注[一]獻帝春秋曰：「[融見]操[曰]：『刑之不濫，君之明也。

translations.literal: [One] Annals of Emperor Xian says: '(Kong Rong saw) Cao (said): 'Punishments not excessive—lord's brightness.
translations.idiomatic: [1] The Annals of Emperor Xian: when Kong Rong saw Cao Cao he said, To keep punishments within bounds shows a ruler's clarity.

sentences.zh: 楊彪獲罪，懼者甚觿。』」

translations.literal: Yang Biao met crime—fearers very xi.
translations.idiomatic: When Yang Biao was charged, many were seized with fear (text variant for the last word).

sentences.zh: 注[二]左傳曰：「康誥曰：『父不慈，子不祗，兄不友，弟不恭，不相及也。

translations.literal: [Two] Zuo Commentary says: 'Kang Gao says: 'Father not kind—son not reverent—elder brother not friendly—younger brother not respectful—not extend to one another.
translations.idiomatic: [2] The Zuo cites the Kang gao: if fathers are unkind, sons irreverent, elder brothers unfriendly, or younger brothers disrespectful, guilt does not spread from one to another.

sentences.zh: 』」注[三]易文言曰：「積善之家，必有餘慶。」

translations.literal: [Three] Changes wen yan says: 'Accumulate good's household—certainly has surplus blessing.
translations.idiomatic: [3] The Wen yan commentary on the Changes: a house that piles up goodness will have blessings left over for its descendants.

sentences.zh: 注[四]說文曰：「纓，冠索也。

translations.literal: [Four] Shuowen says: 'Ying—cap cord.
translations.idiomatic: [4] The Shuowen defines ying as the cord of a cap.

sentences.zh: 」鄭玄注禮記曰：「緌，冠飾也。

translations.literal: ' Zheng Xuan annotated Record of Rites says: 'Rui—cap ornament.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan on the Record of Rites: rui is the tassel or fringe at the cap's brim.

sentences.zh: 紳，帶也。

translations.literal: Shen—sash.
translations.idiomatic: Shen is the sash.

sentences.zh: 搢，插也，插笏於紳也。

translations.literal: Jin—insert—insert tablet at sash.
translations.idiomatic: Jin means to insert, as when one slips the tablet into the sash.

sentences.zh: 」或作「縉」者，淺赤，言帶之色。

translations.literal: ' Or write 'jin'—shallow red—speaks sash's color.
translations.idiomatic: Some manuscripts read jin for a light red that describes the sash.

sentences.zh: 注[五]左傳曰，季文子謂晉韓穿曰：「四方諸侯，誰不解體！

translations.literal: [Five] Zuo Commentary says—Ji Wenzi told Jin Han Chuan saying: 'Four quarters marquises—who not loosen body!
translations.idiomatic: [5] The Zuo: Ji Wenzi said to Han Chuan of Jin, Which of the feudal lords is not limp with terror?

sentences.zh: 」杜預注曰：「言不復肅敬也。」

translations.literal: ' Du Yu commentary says: 'Speak not again solemn respect.
translations.idiomatic: Du Yu: they could no longer maintain solemn reverence.

sentences.zh: 注[六]若以非罪殺彪，融則還為魯國一男子，不復更來朝也。

translations.literal: [Six] If with not-crime kill Biao—Rong then return be Lu state one man—not again further come court.
translations.idiomatic: [6] If Yang Biao were executed without guilt, Kong Rong would go home a mere commoner of Lu and never attend court again.

sentences.zh: 四年，復拜太常，十年免。

translations.literal: Fourth year—again appointed Grand Master of Ceremonies—tenth year dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year he was again appointed grand master of ceremonies; in the tenth year he was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 十一年，諸以恩澤為侯者皆奪封。

translations.literal: Eleventh year—those who by grace favor became marquises all stripped enfeoffment.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh year everyone who had been enfeoffed as a marquis purely by favor lost the title.

sentences.zh: [一]彪見漢祚將終，遂稱恭攣不復行，積十年。

translations.literal: [One] Biao saw Han fortune about end—thereupon claimed foot cramp not again walk—accumulated ten years.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Seeing that Han was near its end, Yang Biao pleaded a foot ailment and stayed away from office for ten years (gongluan may be a scribal form for lameness).

sentences.zh: 後子修為曹操所殺，操見彪問曰：「公何瘦之甚？

translations.literal: Later son Xiu by Cao Cao killed—Cao saw Biao asked saying: 'Lord how emaciated so extreme?
translations.idiomatic: Later his son Yang Xiu was put to death by Cao Cao. When Cao saw Yang Biao he asked, Why have you grown so thin?

sentences.zh: 」對曰：「愧無日磾先見之明，猶懷老牛舐犢之愛。

translations.literal: ' Answered saying: 'Ashamed lack Jin Midi foresight's brightness—still cherish old cow licks calf's love.
translations.idiomatic: He replied, I lack Jin Midi's foresight, yet I still feel the old cow's love that licks her calf.

sentences.zh: 」[二]操為之改容。

translations.literal: ' [Two] Cao for it changed countenance.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Cao Cao's expression softened at that.

sentences.zh: 注[一]彪父賜，以師傅封臨晉侯。

translations.literal: [One] Biao's father Ci—because tutor mentor enfeoffed Linjin marquis.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Yang Biao's father Yang Ci was enfeoffed as marquis of Linjin for serving as imperial tutor.

sentences.zh: 注[二]前書曰，金日磾子二人，武帝所愛，以為弄兒。

translations.literal: [Two] Former Documents says—Jin Midi's sons two persons—Emperor Wu loved—made play children.
translations.idiomatic: [2] The Han shu relates that Emperor Wu doted on two sons of Jin Midi and kept them as his playmates.

sentences.zh: 其後弄兒壯大，不謹，自殿下與宮人戲，日磾適見之，惡其淫亂，遂殺弄兒。

translations.literal: Thereafter play children grown large—not careful—from hall below with palace women play—Midi happened saw them—hated their lewd chaos—thereupon killed play children.
translations.idiomatic: When the boys grew careless and sported with palace women below the hall, Jin Midi caught them, detested their debauchery, and killed them himself.

sentences.zh: 彪子修

translations.literal: Biao's son Xiu
translations.idiomatic: Yang Biao's son: Yang Xiu

sentences.zh: 修字德祖，好學，有俊才，為丞相曹操主簿，[一]用事曹氏。

translations.literal: Xiu styled Dezu—liked learning—had outstanding talent—became Chancellor Cao Cao chief clerk; [one] wielded affairs Cao clan.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Xiu, courtesy name Dezu, loved learning and had rare ability; he became chief clerk to Chancellor Cao Cao and handled business for the Cao house.

sentences.zh: 及操自平漢中，欲因討劉備而不得進，欲守之又難為功，護軍不知進止何依。
操於是出教，唯曰「雞肋」而已。
外曹莫能曉，修獨曰：「夫雞肋，食之則無所得，□之則如可惜，公歸計決矣。
」乃令外白稍嚴，操於此回師。
修之幾決，多有此類。
修又嘗出行，籌操有問外事，乃逆為荅記，□守舍兒：「若有令出，依次通之。
」既而果然。
如是者三，操怪其速，使廉之，知狀，[二]於此忌修。
且以袁術之甥，慮為後患，遂因事殺之。
注[一]典略曰：「修，建安中舉孝廉，除郎中，丞相請署倉曹屬主簿。
是時軍國多事，修總知內外事，皆稱意。
自魏太子以下，並爭與交好。」
注[二]廉，察也。
注[三]續漢書曰：「人有白修與臨淄侯曹植飲醉共載，從司馬門出，謗訕鄢陵侯章。
太祖聞之大怒，故遂收殺之，時年四十五矣。」
修所著賦、頌、碑、贊、詩、哀辭、表、記、書凡十五篇。
及魏文帝受禪，欲以彪為太尉，先遣使示旨。
彪辭曰：「彪備漢三公，遭世傾亂，不能有所補益。
耄年被病，豈可讚惟新之朝？
」遂固辭。
乃授光祿大夫，賜几杖衣袍，[一]因朝會引見，令彪著布單衣、鹿皮冠，杖而入，待以賓客之禮。
年八十四，黃初六年卒於家。
自震至彪，四世太尉，德業相繼，與袁氏俱為東京名族雲。
注[一]續漢書曰「魏文帝詔曰：『先王制几杖之賜，所以賓禮黃耇。
太尉楊彪，乃祖以來世著名績，其賜公延年杖。
延請之日便使杖入』」也。
注[二]華嶠書曰：「東京楊氏、袁氏，累世宰相，為漢名族。
然袁氏車馬衣服極為奢僭；
能守家風，為世所貴，不及楊氏也。」
論曰：孔子稱「危而不持，顛而不扶，則將焉用彼相矣」。
[一]誠以負荷之寄，不可以虛冒，[二]崇高之位，憂重責深也。
延、光之閒，震為上相，抗直方以臨權枉，[三] 先公道而後身名，可謂懷王臣之節，[四]識所任之體矣。
遂累葉載德，[五]繼踵宰相。
信哉，「積善之家，必有餘慶」。
先世韋、平，方之蔑矣。
注[一]論語載孔子之言也。
相扶持者，諭臣當輔君也。
注[二]負荷之寄，周公、霍光之儔。
注[三]坤六二曰「直方大不習無不利」也。
注[四]易曰：「王臣謇謇，匪躬之故。」
注[五]易曰：「德積載。
」載，重也。
注[六]韋賢、平當父子並相繼為丞相。
贊曰：楊氏載德，仍世柱國。
[一]震畏四知，秉去三惑。
賜亦無諱，彪誠匪忒。
[二]修雖才子，渝我淳則。
注[一]言世為國柱臣也。
注[二]忒，差也。
注[三]渝，變也。

一七五九頁三行八世祖喜按：集解引惠棟說，謂太尉楊震碑作「熹」，喜讀為熹也。
一七六０頁四行續漢*
**[書]*曰集解引沉欽韓說，謂「志」當作「書」，今據改。
按：御覽九百九十六引作「謝承後漢書」。
一七六一頁二行倫字仲桓按：集解引惠棟說，謂案儒林傳。
倫字仲理，東昏人。
倫理名字相副，作「桓」者未詳。
一七六三頁八行篡辭也按：「辭」原斗「亂」，逕據汲本、殿本改正。
一七六五頁四行去年十*
**[二]*月四日京師地動按：延光二年十二月戊辰，京師及郡國三地震。
通鑒考異謂下文「其日戊辰」，十一月丙申朔，戊辰乃十二月四日也。
今據改。
一七六六頁四行小人怨詈則還自敬德汲本「還自敬德」作「皇自敬德」，腢書治要作「洗目改聽」。
按：李慈銘謂案無逸「皇自敬德」今文尚書作「況自敬德」，隸釋載漢熹平石經尚書殘碑「況」作「兄」，兄即古況字，王肅尚書注訓為滋益。
石經用今文，楊震受歐陽尚書，故此疏用今文作「況自敬德」，因誤作「洗目改聽」，皆因形近致鬥。
章懷注僅引古文尚書「皇自敬德」，後人不解「況」字，遂改作「還」字，幸治要四字皆誤，轉可推求而得。
一七六六頁一四行震行至城西幾陽亭汲本、殿本「幾」作「夕」。
集解引惠棟說，謂東觀記作「洛陽都亭」，袁宏紀作「洛陽沉亭」，通鑒作「幾陽亭」。
今按：清胡克家翻刻元刊胡注本通鑒作「夕陽亭」，章鈺校宋刊本通鑒三種及明孔天胤本，並作「幾陽亭」。
一七六八頁一０行帝嘗從容問奇曰按：「嘗」原作「常」，逕據汲本、殿本改。
一七六九頁六行桃林縣有蓩鄉按：「桃林」當作「弘農」。
集解引惠棟說，謂郡國志宏農郡宏農縣有桃邱聚，故桃林，有蓩鄉。
桃林非縣名，注訛。
一七六九頁八行秉字叔節按：校補引柳從辰說，謂御覽二百七引張璠漢記作「字叔卿」。
一七六九頁一二行私過幸河南尹梁胤府捨按：集解引沉欽韓說，謂袁宏紀雲幸梁不疑府，梁冀子為河南尹在元嘉初元之後，袁紀是。
一七七０頁四行特蒙哀識集解引王補說，謂袁紀「哀識」作「光識」。
按：校補謂「哀」字疑當作「表」。
一七七０頁五行太尉黃瓊按：校補引柳從辰說，謂「太尉」袁紀作「太常」，又袁紀載秉上疏在元嘉元年，而瓊為太尉在永興二年，則作「太常」是也。
一七七０頁一六行日月星辰山龍華蟲藻火粉米*[黼黻]*十二章據汲本、殿本補。
一七七一頁二行居郎閒汲本、殿本「郎」斗「廊」。
按：前書顏注，郎著皁衣，故章玄服以廁也。
一七七一頁七行中常侍單超弟匡按：集解引錢大昕說，謂案第五種傳以匡為超兄子，宦者傳以為超弟子。
一七七二頁一行可告在所屬按：刊誤謂案文多一「在」字。
一七七四頁四行覽顧知釁重汲本、殿本「顧」作「固」。
按：顧固通。
一七七四頁七行投畀*
**[豺]*虎刊誤謂「有」當作「豺」，注無它說，知與詩同。
今據改。
一七七五頁一０行左傳曰晉寺人披言也「言」原斗「吉」，逕改正。
按：「曰」字疑衍。
一七七五頁一五行賜字伯獻按：集解引惠棟說，謂太尉楊公碑及文烈楊公碑皆云字伯猷，袁宏紀字子猷。
又引沉欽韓說，謂謝承書作「伯欽」。
又校補引柳從辰說，謂今袁紀作「字子獻」，又東觀記作「字伯獻」，與此同。
一七七七頁六行布恩施惠按：「惠」原斗「志」，逕改正。
一七七七頁一四行景公時按：陳景雲謂「景公」上脫「宋」字。
一七七八頁一行周文日□不暇汲本、殿本「□」作「昃」。
按：昃本作□，□為□之或字。
一七七八頁一一行文王自朝至於日中仄汲本、殿本「仄」作「昃」。
按：仄昃通。
一七七九頁五行長壯大節集解引沉欽韓說，謂列女傳「壯」作「指」。
今按：初學記引作「壯」。
一七七九頁六行折□出匈汲本、殿本「出」作「凸」。
按：列女傳作「出」，初學記引同。
一七七九頁六行年四十按：集解引沉欽韓說，謂「四十」新序及初學記並作「三十」。
一七七九頁六行行嫁不售按：集解引沉欽韓說，謂列女傳「行」作「衒」。
一七八一頁四行禹數視其少子按：「少」原斗「小」，逕改正。
一七八一頁九行檮杌次於*
**[丕]*山據殿本改。
一七八一頁一二行蝀音董按：汲本「董」作「東」。
一七八三頁四行齊宣五里按：集解引惠棟說，謂王懋雲世說舉樂松之語，雲齊五十里，乃知非五里也，當時史文於「五」字下脫一「十」字。
蓋七十里近於百里，四十里近於五十里。
樂松舉其大要耳。
一七八五頁二行張*
**[酺]*曾孫據校補引柳從辰說改。
按：張濟為張酺曾孫，已見酺傳。
一七八六頁三行三百石長導從置門下五吏賊曹功曹皆帶□車道按：刊誤謂案後漢志文，此不合有「三百石長」四字。
又云「賊曹、督盜賊、功曹皆帶□，三車導」，此文少「督盜賊」三字，又少一「三」字，又誤「導」字也。
蓋門下五吏，賊曹一，督盜賊一，功曹一，主簿一，主記一，凡五車也。
一七八六頁一三行光武宮洛陽於今亦十世矣按：沈家本謂魏志董卓傳注「十世」作「十一世」，是也。
此奪「一」也。
一七八六頁一五行*[昔]*關中遭王莽變亂據汲本、殿本補。
一七八七頁一二行及車駕還洛陽按：「還」原斗「遷」，逕改正。
史記曰：「喜追殺項羽，以功封」
